Creatures born of intense pain and suffering, the armless, six-legged misery siktemporas are some of the most numerous of their kind since misery is as ubiquitous an experience as living. They're also the most proactive of siktemporas, seeking to propagate the misery from which they spawn. They delight in opportunities to travel to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), where misery is so woefully prevalent and easily multiplied. **[Recall Knowledge - Time](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Misery Siktempora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1294) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 232 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 300 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Dimension of Time Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +5 **Temporal Sense** A siktempora can always roll Dimension of Time Lore for initiative. When it does, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to the roll. --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +23 **Will** +25 **HP** 160 , regeneration 40 (deactivated by positive, mental, or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378)) **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Slash the Suffering** Creatures with the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) condition are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the misery siktempora.
**Uncanny Pounce** **Trigger** The misery siktempora rolls Dimension of Time Lore for initiative; **Effect** The misery siktempora Strides once and makes a claw Strike, in either order. --- **Speed** 35 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Melee** claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+13 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 29 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (×3) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **6th** [Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27) - **Constant (6th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Inflict Misery** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** The misery siktempora's last action was a claw Strike that damaged the target; **Effect** The misery siktempora fills the target's thoughts with abject despair. The target must attempt a DC 32 Will save. Regardless of outcome, the target is then immune to Inflict Misery for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), and [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but for 1 minute. **Telepathic Wail** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The misery siktempora assails the mind of one creature within 100 feet with a wail of anguish and torment that deals 8d6 mental damage (DC 32 basic Will save). On a failed save, the target is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for the first action of its next turn (or for 1 round on a critical failure).
name: Misery Siktempora

markdown:
Triumph siktemporas embody the emotional satisfaction of overcoming a task that took up much of a creature's mind, be it taking an apple seed from between one's teeth or winning a war. They exult in every victory they attain; however, they value only those successes won fairly not only abiding by the established rules of a competition but also enforcing those rules mercilessly upon other competitors. These siktemporas appear as small humanoids with four muscular arms and digitigrade legs. They take great joy in draping themselves in golden clothes and medals that commemorate the countless victories which spawned them, and their faces bear proud, dazzling smiles and expressions of success—but these features never move. **[Recall Knowledge - Time](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Triumph Siktempora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1295) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 233 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 200 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Dimension of Time Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +22, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Temporal Sense** A siktempora can always roll Dimension of Time Lore for initiative. When it does, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to the roll. --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +24 **Will** +26 **HP** 190 , regeneration 40 (deactivated by chaos, mental, or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378)) **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Cheaters Never Prosper** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 50 feet. A creature within the aura who uses or benefits from an effect with the [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) trait must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round (slowed 2 on a critical failure).
**Clinch Victory** **Trigger** A creature within the triumph siktempora's reach uses an action that would restore Hit Points; **Effect** The triumph siktempora makes a glorious fist Strike against the triggering creature. On a critical success, this Strike also disrupts the triggering action.
**Uncanny Pounce** **Trigger** The triumph siktempora rolls Dimension of Time Lore for initiative; **Effect** The triumph siktempora Strides once and makes a claw Strike, in either order. --- **Speed** 60 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Melee** glorious fist +30, **Damage** 3d12+16 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 32 - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **7th** [Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27) - **Constant (7th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Vie for Victory** The triumph siktempora pushes themself beyond their limits to secure victory over a chosen foe. They choose one target they can see and reach with a single Stride action. They attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) that target, ignoring the typical limitations on distance and with no penalty for not sharing a language with the target. They then Stride and attempt one [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15) action (such as [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) or [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41)) against the target. **Winning Smile** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The triumph siktempora flashes their disarming grin at one creature within 15 feet. The target takes 10d8 mental damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). On a failure, the target's attacks against the triumph siktempora also become [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) until the end of the target's next turn.
Triumphant Appearances Triumph siktemporas find purpose and a sense of self in winning and victory, so when a war rages across the land and one side struggles behind the rest, a triumph siktempora might venture to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) to join the side of the underdog and grasp victory—or die trying! The same can happen for a major sporting event or even an intense legal battle where a persuasive voice could turn the tide. All that matters to them is victory
name: Triumph Siktempora

markdown:
Acts of great and intense love spawn love siktemporas, kindly creatures bearing intense strength that start and end wars in the name of the love that birthed them. These adept combatants form crossbows and bolts out of their emotional bonds, though they typically fight only to protect that which they love. While unable to speak given their featureless faces, they're nevertheless compassionate listeners and savvy communicators. Each love siktempora takes the form of two small humanoids conjoined at the hip with three legs, two arms, and two heads. Their faces are completely featureless except for a faint, ever present blush spread across their cheeks, and they always appear garbed in stylish garments from obscure civilizations. **[Recall Knowledge - Time](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Love Siktempora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1296) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 233 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 500 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Dimension of Time Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +28, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +31, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +26, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +33 **Str** +4 **Dex** +9 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 **Telepathic Singer** A love siktempora can provide verbal components and auditory performances for their [composition](/Traits.aspx?ID=31) spells even though they can't speak. They do so without needing to make noise, as all creatures within range of their telepathy hear the components in their mind. However, the siktempora can do so only as long as at least one other creature with an Intelligence modifier of +0 or higher is within the range of their telepathy.
**Temporal Sense** A siktempora can always roll Dimension of Time Lore for initiative. When it does, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to the roll. --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +31 **Will** +28 **HP** 210 , regeneration 50 (deactivated by evil, mental, or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378)) **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Deflect Aggression** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** The love siktempora is targeted by an attack; **Effect** The siktempora glows with compassion, deflecting the attacker's aggression. The siktempora gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their AC and resistance 15 against the triggering attack, and the attacker must attempt a saving throw against the siktempora's heartsong (see below).
**Uncanny Pounce** **Trigger** The love siktempora rolls Dimension of Time Lore for initiative; **Effect** The love siktempora Strides once and makes a claw Strike, in either order. --- **Speed** 50 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Ranged** emotional bolt +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [range increment 80 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d10+10 piercing plus 1d10 mental and heartsong **Occult Innate Spells** DC 37 - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **8th** [Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27) - **Constant (8th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Bard Composition Spells** DC 37, 3 Focus Points - **Cantrips (8th)** [Inspire Courage](/Spells.aspx?ID=386), [Inspire Defense](/Spells.aspx?ID=387) - **8th** [Lingering Composition](/Spells.aspx?ID=389) **Heartsong** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A creature that takes mental damage from the love siktempora's emotional bolt must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the siktempora until the end of the target's next turn. [Hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) don't end this fascinated condition. **Love's Impunity** The love siktempora's emotional bolt Strike doesn't trigger reactions that normally trigger based off making a ranged attack. **Telepathic Ballad** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The love siktempora casts [_inspire courage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=386) or [_inspire defense_](/Spells.aspx?ID=387). It increases the area to a 200-foot emanation and increases the status bonuses the cantrip provides to +2.
name: Love Siktempora

markdown:
Spawned by acts and legacies of cold-blooded spite, hatred siktemporas kill indiscriminately out of pure loathing, methodically hunting down and slaughtering anyone who catches their vile attention. Fortunately for mortals, they typically limit their predations to the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=21), where they hunt down creatures foreign to that realm—and some native to it. Hatred siktemporas appear roughly humanoid, but their gaunt forms resemble bone and tendon more than flesh and skin. Adding to their unnerving appearance, their legs have an additional segment between their shin and thigh, and likewise for their eerily long arms. Their featureless faces bear only a singular scar, which takes a different form on every hatred siktempora. They clad themselves in immaculate finery chosen from the most vitriolic empires that ever existed, in the past and the future, and they form a magical garrote as a manifestation of their cold malice. **[Recall Knowledge - Time](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Hatred Siktempora](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1297) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 235 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language), [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 500 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +32, [Dimension of Time Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +32 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 **Temporal Sense** A siktempora can always roll Dimension of Time Lore for initiative. When it does, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to the roll. --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +30 **Will** +33 **HP** 240 , regeneration 50 (deactivated by good, mental, or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378)) **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Impending Dread** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 150 feet. Creatures within the hatred siktempora's aura don't reduce the value of their [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition automatically at the end of their turns.
**Uncanny Pounce** **Trigger** The hatred siktempora rolls Dimension of Time Lore for initiative; **Effect** The hatred siktempora Strides once and makes a claw Strike, in either order.
**Punish Flight** **Trigger** A creature attempts to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) from the hatred siktempora; **Effect** The siktempora makes a loathing garotte Strike against the triggering creature. This Strike doesn't have [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27). --- **Speed** 80 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Melee** loathing garotte +35 ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 cold plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 40 - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **9th** [Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27) - **Constant (9th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Contorted Clutch** The hatred siktempora can have up to two creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) with their loathing garotte at once. **Vindictive Crush** **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The hatred siktempora has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The hatred siktempora tightens their garrote, dealing 5d10 bludgeoning damage to each creature they're Grabbing. Each creature grabbed by the siktempora must attempt a DC 40 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and can't breathe or speak while it remains grabbed; it must hold its breath or begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but double damage and the creature becomes [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) and can't speak for 1 round after it's no longer grabbed.
name: Hatred Siktempora

This troop of skeletons was once a cohort of highly disciplined spear-and-shield infantry from an ancient empire. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Skeleton Infantry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1300) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 237 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +18 **Will** +19 **HP** 180 (16 squares) **Resistances** cold 5, electricity 5, fire 5, piercing 10, slashing 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 8 **Form a Phalanx** Many of the skeletons raise their shields to protect others. The infantry gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of their next turn.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; troop movement **Lower Spears!** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The skeletons engage in a coordinated longspear attack against each enemy within 10 feet (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d8 piercing damage
3d8+8 piercing damage
4d8+8 piercing damage **Hurl Javelins!** The troop's members throw a volley of javelins. Each creature in a 10-foot burst within 30 feet of the troop takes 2d6+10 piercing damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the troop is reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)** **Phalanx Charge** **Requirements** The infantry is in a phalanx; **Effect** The skeletons lower their longspears and charge. The troop Strides in a straight line until they're adjacent to an enemy then use Lower Spears!, dealing 3d8+8 piercing damage. Any creature that fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). **Troop Movement** Whenever the skeleton infantry Stride, they first [Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43) as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then move up to their Speed. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving; for instance, if any square of the infantry enter difficult terrain, the extra movement cost applies to all the guards.
name: Skeleton Infantry

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Street Skelm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1301)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Street skelms abuse the deference given to elders and the well-to-do in public spaces, appearing as such so they can verbally or physically punish those who dare defy their cruel sensibilities. To excuse his actions or gather allies, a street skelm might rant about a target, building a pretense of some vague threat the target poses to the community's social order. These rantings can rally a mob to carry out the skelm's violence against his target, some of whom even act out of fear against becoming the skelm's next target. Street skelms have little of the magical talents possessed by their more powerful peers, mostly relying on brute force.

 Street skelms typically dress in old-fashioned styles, always including a hat or hood that can cover their distinctive features. They often walk with a cane, walking staff, or other prop suitable for bludgeoning those who offend their delicate egos or challenge their tenuous sense of control.

<column gap="tiny">

**Frightening Rant** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The skelm rants angrily, filling nearby creatures with shame, fear, and anger. Each creature within 30 feet must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 2 on a critical failure). While frightened by this rant, creatures take a –2 status penalty to Strength-based damage rolls against the skelm, but gain a +2 status bonus to those rolls against other creatures.

**Punishing Strike** <actions string="Single Action" />  The skelm unleashes his constant rage in a reckless attack that makes him [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of his next turn. He Strikes; if he hits, he increases the number of damage dice by one and adds [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) to the effect. This counts as two attacks for his multiple attack penalty.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Shrine Skelm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1302)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The unquestioned authority that a priest wields, with influence over even the most intimate details of a congregation's lives, is the ultimate prize for a shrine skelm. Most shrine skelms insinuate themselves into positions of judgment in powerful religious groups to gain that power for their own use and abuse. Shrine skelms wear the old-fashioned, traditional apparel of whatever faith they've infiltrated, brandishing religious symbols made of precious metals to better blend in and give an impression of age and power.

 No matter what religion they infiltrate, a shrine skelm is a master at taking established teachings out of context to justify elaborate, painful, and often humiliating punishments for his victims. He usually targets people with the audacity to question his position or teachings, but also relentlessly harasses anyone he envies.

 Shrine skelms are skilled listeners, especially in settings like group prayers where they can learn worshippers' fears or hopes. They exploit whatever information they learn for their own prestige and power, but they also watch for jealousy and the seeds of rage that could be nurtured to make a new skelm. They can even intercept sincere prayers and steal their effects for themselves, and they command several spells to manipulate and control their unwitting, vulnerable victims.

Skelms crave power, and the palace skelms who stalk the halls where such power concentrates are among the most dangerous of their kind. They assume titles that allow them maximum freedom to punish or hurt others with minimal oversight: any vindictive guard captain, tyrannical viceroy, or needlessly cruel magistrate could be or become a palace skelm. They delight in the wealth and status of their positions, garbing themselves in ornate finery that reflects their station. Palace skelms ingratiate themselves with powerful individuals and gather followers by stoking fears of losing long-held or hard-earned power—especially power gained through illicit means. They undermine their enemies by encouraging competition, jealousy, and outright paranoia by way of magic and false messages. These skelms possess an unnatural ability to twist spoken words and worm their manipulative magic into others' speech, sowing confusion and hatred that might explode into violence. A political upheaval is a palace skelm's greatest fear and opportunity alike, upsetting the balance of power he has built but providing countless new opportunities to sow hatred and evil. This conflicting goal leads palace skelms to self-sabotage and undermine their own efforts more than any other skelm. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Palace Skelm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1303) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 240 **Perception** +15; [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +16, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** signet ring --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +16 **Will** +15 –2 to all saves vs. [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects **HP** 155 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 **Corrupt Speech** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** The skelm hears a creature speak within 30 feet; **Effect** The skelm sows paranoia by putting treacherous words on another's lips. The skelm whispers up to 12 words and attempts a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against the Perception DC of a creature other than the triggering creature within 30 feet.
**Critical Success** The target hears the skelm's words as if they were spoken by the triggering creature. This can alter [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) effects. The skelm also casts [_paranoia_](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) or [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) on the target, if he likes.
**Success** As critical success, except the skelm can't cast _paranoia_ or _suggestion_.
**Failure** The target doesn't hear the skelm's words, and they have no effect.
**Critical Failure** The target hears the skelm speak the words. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** antler +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d12+9 piercing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (×3) - **4th** [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=39), [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) (at will), [Private Sanctum](/Spells.aspx?ID=235), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Rituals** DC 26 - **2nd** [Inveigle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=14) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The palace skelm can take on the appearance of any Medium male [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91). This doesn't change his Speed or his attack and damage bonuses with his Strikes but might change the damage type his Strikes deal. **Incite Violence** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The skelm gives an impassioned speech calling for his followers to act upon their convictions. Each creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 24 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature can immediately Strike an adjacent creature of its choosing.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature immediately Strikes an adjacent creature; if multiple creatures are adjacent, the skelm chooses the target. If no creatures are adjacent, the creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) and [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with the skelm until the start of its next turn.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Soul Skelm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1304)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Soul skelms arise not from mortal men but from other skelms, as their long-held wrath slowly twists them and replaces lingering fragments of their mortality with even more fiendish aspects. These skelms bully and exploit the souls of the dead, binding souls and undead to keep themselves indefinitely young. They bully living victims as well, stoking regret and shame even from inconsequential minutiae or outright fabrications until these feelings become unbreakable ties that the skelm can leverage from beyond the grave. A soul skelm's attentions can leave a victim isolated from friends and allies, making them all the more susceptible to the skelm's manipulations.

 Ambitious soul skelms—often those who arise from [palace skelms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1303)—search out powerful, high-strung mortals and trick them into bringing ridicule or shame on themselves. After their victim's death, the skelms gleefully remind them of their mortal failings, feeding on their despair.

This doesn't change his Speed or his attack and damage bonuses with his Strikes but might change the damage type his Strikes deal.

**Isolating Lash** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The soul skelm makes a melee Strike trailing a wave of shadowy illusions. The skelm is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of his next turn. If the Strike is successful, the skelm increases the number of damage dice by one, and the target must attempt a DC 29 Will save. On a failure, the target becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), inaudible, and otherwise imperceptible to its allies for 4 rounds, and it likewise can't see, hear, or otherwise perceive those allies. Regardless of the outcome, the creature is temporarily immune to Isolating Lash for 24 hours.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Skinstitch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1305)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Skinstitches' hulking frames are made from flayed humanoid skin stuffed with straw and poisonous insects. The dark god [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=13) grants the secrets for creating these monstrosities in disturbing dreams he sends to select adherents, in which the dreamer imagines themself skinning a beloved pet, friend, or family member. Those that accept these teachings become skinstitchers. The creation of a skinstitch is a crude process compared to the making of a more advanced construct like a [golem](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=57). Rough stitching, imprecise shapes, and shoddy materials give a skinstitch its patchwork appearance.

 While most skinstitches have vaguely humanoid shapes, they often appear monstrous due to various modifications their creators have made to their bodies. Some skinstitches' arms, for instance, might bear jagged metal blades or blunt iron rods. The most talented skinstitchers rarely bother with the humanoid form at all and instead opt to craft skinstitches that resemble other creatures sacred to Norgorber, such as scorpions, centipedes, or spiders. Rumors lurk of skinstitchers who can use exotic materials like steel, daemon flesh, or even magical shadows for their creations.

 The rotting materials used to build skinstitches tend to attract vermin, which can in turn form a hive or nest within the creations' bodies. Skinstitchers consider the presence of such tenants to be a sure sign of Norgorber's approval.

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

**Pest Haven** The rotting hides and molding straw stuffing of skinstitches are ideal nesting grounds for vermin and insect swarms. Any [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) [swarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=239) sharing a space with a skinstitch gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) 3. Additionally, if a creature deals at least 10 piercing or slashing damage to the skinstitch at once, the swarm can use its swarming bites Strike (or similar attack) against the attacker as a reaction.

**Stitch Skin** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The skinstitch hasn't used this ability since the last time it successfully used its Flay ability; **Effect** The skinstitch sews flayed flesh to its body to seal tears and rents. The skinstitch regains 8 Hit Points.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Skitterstitch</title>

Skitterstitches are eight-legged skinstitches crafted to resemble giant spiders. These elite skinstitch variants have different attacks from the standard [skinstitch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=994), including a deadly poison. Skitterstitches always house [spider swarms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=385) within their bodies, which scuttle forth when their host is destroyed.  

</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Skull Peeler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1306)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Skull peelers, despite their ferocious and well-deserved moniker, are considered by many to be downright adorable, especially when viewed from a safe distance. Their soft, dappled brown fur helps them hide in forest canopies, and they have shimmering insectile wings and big eyes that draw in the faintest beams of light. At first glance, a skull peeler looks like a cuddly pet or a wizard's familiar. Any illusions of domesticating such a beast are quickly dismissed upon seeing how a skull peeler eats, however.

 Skull peelers have evolved to hang motionlessly in treetop canopies as they wait until appropriate prey appears, usually long-necked dinosaurs but also brachiating primates and large birds. The skull peeler then lashes out with its long tongue, severing the creature's head from its body and pulling the detached cranium back into its hungry paws. It then uses its claws to crack open the cranial cavity—hence its name—before lapping up the tasty insides.

 Despite skull peelers' gruesome eating habits, some enterprises and individuals can't resist the urge to add these beasts to their menageries. Fey and other creatures with ties to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), such as [gnomes](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=3), can occasionally coax a skull peeler into a form of domestication. While the adorable beasts can never be fully tamed due to their hyper-evolved hunting instincts, they can be bribed with food and, if kept satiated, displayed on a perch or indoor terrarium as an example of their master's cunning and deadliness. As often as not, these pseudo-domesticated skull peelers end up devouring a guest, pet, or their would-be owner, but this possibility doesn't stop up-and-coming crime lords from attempting to domesticate the little predators. Skull peelers kept in well-managed zoos fare somewhat better, but these clever creatures don't always stay in their cages, which has led to wild skull peelers in places travelers might not expect.

<column gap="tiny">

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22

</column>

</row>

</row>

**Snatch Skull** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The skull peeler is using Perfect Camouflage and a creature moves into a space within 15 feet of it; **Effect** The skull peeler [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) toward the triggering creature and Strikes with its tongue. If this Strike is successful, the skull peeler automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target with its tongue.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, climb 15 feet, fly 10 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d4+8 slashing plus 1d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +17,
**Damage** 2d10+8 slashing

**Anticoagulant**   The skull peeler's razor-sharp tongue is coated in an anticoagulant substance that makes wounds it inflicts particularly hard to close. The DC of the flat check to end the [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from a skull peeler's tongue is 16, or 11 with appropriate assistance.

**Perfect Camouflage** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Requirements** The skull peeler is in a treetop or standing on a tree limb; **Effect** Until the next time it acts, the skull peeler hangs perfectly still, blending into the treetop surroundings. It has an automatic result of 36 on [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks and DCs to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) from any creature more than 10 feet away from it.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">A Grim Nature</title>

While explorers and adventurers who encounter skull peelers in the wild often assume the small beasts resulted from some ill-advised magical experiment by a foolish wizard, they're actually a naturally occurring species. Though their unusual assembly of characteristics might carry some minor [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) influence, skull peelers evolved from other tree-dwelling mammals over thousands of years. In their current form, they're successful predators in their environment, mostly feeding on long-necked dinosaurs—prey that other, much larger carnivores still struggle to bring down!
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Feral Skull Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1308)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Some skull swarms are crafted from the heads of feral and dangerous beasts. These can include predatory cats, bears, [dinosaurs](/Traits.aspx?ID=45), and the like, but just as often they're made from bulls, horses, apes, and other creatures that were gentler in life. In death, a feral skull swarm is bloodthirsty regardless of its origins, with a keen scent for living flesh. Even herbivore skulls diligently chew on the flesh of their swarm's victims.

The act of creating a [demilich](/Monsters.aspx?ID=283) sometimes goes horribly awry, leaving behind a shattered remnant of a once-mighty spellcaster constantly leaking eldritch energies. The ruined spirit animates a cloud of debased copies of itself, glittering with cracked crystals in crude imitation, and it hungers constantly for the magical energies denied it in its corrupted immortality. Some of the spellcaster's personality remains in the swarm, albeit usually unnaturally split between the skulls. As a consequence, the skulls argue among themselves as warring parts of the personality clamor to be heard, resulting in the swarm's fractious, inconstant attitude. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Sorcerous Skull Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1309) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 245 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +28, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +28 **Str** -3 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +8 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +26 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 190 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** bludgeoning 6, cold 12, electricity 12, fire 12, piercing 12, slashing 12 **Weaknesses** area damage 12, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 12 **Boneshard Burst** When a sorcerous skull swarm is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it erupts in an explosion of foul energy and bone fragments in a 30-foot burst, dealing 3d12 piercing damage and 3d12 negative damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save)
**Consume Spell** **Trigger** The swarm is targeted with a spell; **Effect** The swarm casts [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell. If it successfully counteracts the spell, it gains temporary Hit Points equal to twice the level of the counteracted spell that last for 1 minute. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +26 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (at will), [Spell Turning](/Spells.aspx?ID=297) - **Constant (7th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Siphon Magic** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The sorcerous skull swarm saps magic from nearby creatures. Any creature with the ability to cast spells (including innate spells) in the swarm's space takes 15d6 mental damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save); on a failure, the creature is also [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (stupefied 3 on a critical failure). **Swarming Gnaw** Each enemy in the swarm's space takes 5d8 piercing damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

These omnivores are most active at twilight, feeding on everything from berries to grubs and insects. Some farmers even encourage the animals to live near their farms to keep destructive pest populations low. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Skunk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1310) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 246 **Perception** +5; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 40 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +5, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +2, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** -4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +4 **Ref** +7 **Will** +3 **HP** 7 --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** jaws +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d8 piercing **Spray Musk** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The skunk propels a stream of acrid musk in a 10-foot line. Each creature in the line must attempt a DC 16 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The target is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The target is sickened 2 and takes a –2 penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks from the horrific odor for 24 hours or until the musk is removed or neutralized, requiring 10 minutes of thorough scrubbing with soap.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Skunk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1311)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Giant skunks thrive in places where their larger size and more potent musk allow them to fend off predators their smaller cousins can't. With larger bodies come more powerful appetites, and giant skunks tend to be aggressive and territorial.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 246

claw +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 slashing

**Spray Blinding Musk** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The giant skunk propels potent, acrid musk in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the line must attempt a DC 17 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected. **Success** The target is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). **Failure** The target is sickened 3. **Critical Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round, becomes sickened 3, and takes a –2 penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks from the horrific odor for 24 hours or until the musk is removed or neutralized, requiring 10 minutes of thorough scrubbing with soap.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Slithering Pit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1312)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A slithering pit is a strange, nearly invisible ooze with an extradimensional space for its digestive system, which mimics the appearance of an acid-filled stone pit. It slowly dissolves its captives in stomach acid until they can be digested. A slithering pit can go weeks without feeding, affording it patience.

 Thriving in dilapidated areas, slithering pits take up positions among the plentiful potholes where they can easily pass for just another blemish. They crawl across ramshackle cobblestone streets and damp underground complexes, waiting for unwary prey to stumble by and fall in.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/SlitheringPit.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Slithering Pit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1312)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
<trait label="Ooze" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=122" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 247

**Perception** +9; no vision, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** -5

**Con** +7

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

**Transparent** A slithering pit is so clear it's difficult to spot. A successful DC 30 Perception check is required to notice a stationary slithering pit, and a creature must be [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519) to attempt this check. A creature that walks into the pit's space might fall into any pit currently in effect due to Dimensional Pit.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 14 (10 from inside the Dimensional Pit)

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 220

</row>

**Immunities**
acid, critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

**Breach Vulnerability** Ingesting an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space like that found in a [bag of holding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249) deals 6d8 force damage to the slithering pit and its occupants. The slithering pit then immediately uses Out You Go.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pseudopod +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Flurry of Pods** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The slithering pit makes a single pseudopod Strike against each target within range it doesn't already have [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20). These attacks count toward the slithering pit's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after all of these attacks.

**Dimensional Pit** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The slithering pit opens an extradimensional, 20-foot-deep pit that covers its own space and all adjacent squares unless they're walls or similar blocking terrain. Any other creature occupying or entering pit spaces must succeed at a DC 22 Reflex save or fall into the pit, taking damage from the fall (typically 10 bludgeoning damage). Any creature grabbed by the ooze falls in and is no longer [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), even if it was outside the pit squares. While a dimensional pit is open, the slithering pit is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), can't be forced to move, and can make pseudopod Strikes originating from the walls of the pit. A creature that starts its turn at the bottom of the pit takes 2d6 acid damage. Climbing the walls of the pit requires a DC 22 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check. When the slithering pit dies, the dimensional pit closes and creatures inside are ejected, with the effects of Out You Go.

**Out You Go** <actions string="Single Action" />  The slithering pit closes all pit spaces it created using Dimensional Pit, ejecting all its occupants onto the ground into random free spaces where the pit opened. Each occupant takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">A Living Pit, Really?</title>

Slithering pit lore is as strange and confusing as the creature itself. Was it created by a wizard in need of a handy garbage disposal? Did it result from some unfortunate accident involving hungry oozes and one [_bag of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249) too many? Why do its insides mimic the appearance of stone, but without the same toughness? Is it some form of camouflage, letting them pass for an oft-ignored hazard? So many questions...
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-79" />
name: Slithering Pit

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Three-Toed Sloth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1313)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The diminutive three-toed sloth is a solitary, tree-dwelling creature. Possessing a gentle nature and unassuming countenance, it has little to defend itself with other than its surprisingly fearsome climbing claws. These sloths are sometimes used as lookouts by secretive druid enclaves, as the symbiotic algae in their fur makes them difficult to spot in tree canopies, though their slow speed leaves them vulnerable on the ground.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Megatherium](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1314)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Megatheriums dwell deep within ancient forests and humid jungles. Fiercely territorial, these immense creatures are often smelt before seen. Though they travel on all fours, megatheriums can reach foliage 20 feet above the forest floor by standing on their hind legs and short, thick tails. Their enormous foreclaws, used to bend tree limbs within reach of their mouths, can cleave a horse in two and crush its rider.

 The musk of the megatherium serves as a warning to potential predators and other megatheriums that they're too close. Adult megatheriums are so territorial that most don't reproduce more than twice in their lifetime. Though the creatures regularly patrol their territory for intruders, they tend to avoid settlements unless food has become scarce. When they do stray into towns, they've been known to devour entire orchards in a day.

<column gap="tiny">

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Rat Snake Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1315)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A solitary snake might be no cause for alarm, but a hissing mass of frenzied snakes can make even seasoned adventurers shudder. Rat snakes can reach lengths of up to 10 feet, and they gather en masse to hibernate as well as to breed. Though nonvenomous, these territorial snakes will strike anything that threatens them.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The frightening mass of shining scales, gleaming eyes, and fangs dripping with venom that comprise a viper swarm has brought an end to many an unlucky adventurer. Normally nocturnal, these notoriously aggressive snakes strike at anything that comes within reach, be it limb or weapon. Their venom is a potent toxin that leaves victims shaky and weak.

Lacking the intelligence of their kin, falcon-headed hieracosphinxes are cowardly, cruel-hearted creatures. They guard their territory ruthlessly against weaker intruders. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Hieracosphinx](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1317) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 250 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Sphinx](/Languages.aspx?ID=35) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +15 **Will** +10 **HP** 70 **[Buck](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=6)** DC 23
**Contingent Glyph** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A hieracosphinx has an innate magical defense similar to a [_contingency_](/Spells.aspx?ID=49). When they take a specific type of energy damage (varying by the individual sphinx), they can use their reaction to have the glyph cast [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=147) on them. The glyph is then dormant for 1 week. A critical success to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about a particular hieracosphinx (30 or higher on the check) reveals which damage type triggers the glyph. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** beak +15, **Damage** 2d10+4 slashing **Melee** claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+4 slashing **Pounce** The hieracosphinx Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If they began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), they remain hidden until after the attack. **Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) The hieracosphinx emits a shrill, ear-piercing shriek. Each non-sphinx in a 60-foot emanation must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude save. Any creature that attempts a save is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The creature is temporarily immune to that hieracosphinx's shriek for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is deafened for 1 minute and [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
Jealous Predators Hieracosphinxes share none of the fondness for riddling talk and clever conversation typical of their more intelligent kin. They nonetheless envy their cousins' power, wisdom, and grace, and sometimes gather in flocks to take down stronger rival sphinxes out of sheer spite and jealousy.
name: Hieracosphinx

markdown:
Towering over their kin, elder sphinxes are the pharaonic masters of their kind. They crave solitude in eternal vigil over sacred spaces, where they honor ancient pacts. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Elder Sphinx](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1318) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 250 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sphinx](/Languages.aspx?ID=35); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Bardic Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +32, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +30 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +8 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +6 **Bardic Lore** Sphinxes are naturally curious, and their love of puzzles and mysteries leads them to gather information on a broad range of topics. Sphinxes have the [Bardic Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=179) bard feat, allowing them to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) on any topic. --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +25 **Will** +31 **HP** 300 --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** claw +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+16 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 37 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **8th** [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=39) (at will), [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=40) (at will), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247) (at will), [Remove Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) (at will), [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276) (see idols of stone below), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) (at will) - **Constant (6th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Rituals** DC 37 - **6th** [Commune](/Rituals.aspx?ID=6) - **7th** [Legend Lore](/Rituals.aspx?ID=15) **Guardian Monolith** The elder sphinx transforms their body into stone or back to flesh. In stone form, the sphinx is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) but has Hardness 14 and gains immunity to bleed, [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) damage, [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42). They don't age or require food, water, or sleep. They can perceive their surroundings and cast their innate spells. [_Stone to flesh_](/Spells.aspx?ID=311) turns the elder sphinx from stone form back to flesh, and [_flesh to stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=123) turns it from flesh form to stone form. **Idols of Stone** When the sphinx casts [_shape stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=276), they can shape the stone into a magical duplicate of themself. When they cast [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=39), [_clairvoyance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=40), [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=173), or [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=356), they can make the effect come from any duplicate, instead of themself. The elder sphinx can concentrate for 1 minute to transfer their consciousness to any duplicate; the sphinx is then in their stone form from Guardian Monolith in that statue. The sphinx can have no more than four duplicate statues at a time. Casting _shape stone_ at that point causes the oldest duplicate to crumble to dust. **Pose a Riddle** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; The sphinx recites a riddle and compels up to 10 creatures within 30 feet to answer (DC 37 Will save). The GM either runs the riddle out of character by timing the players' attempts, or picks an appropriate associated skill for the riddle, such as [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) for a riddle involving divine mysteries. The sphinx gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls against any creature that answers incorrectly even once. This bonus lasts for 1 day.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, but it can choose to try to help solve the riddle..
**Success** The creature attempts to answer the riddle. Each round spending at least one action attempting a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) check with the chosen skill (or working on the answer, if using an out of game riddle). This lasts for 1 minute, until the creature successfully answers the riddle, or until an enemy takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the creature, whichever comes first.
**Failure** As success but the creature must spend at least two actions each round attempting to answer.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but up to 1 hour. **Pounce** The elder sphinx Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If they began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), they remain hidden until after the attack. **Warding Glyph** Once per day, an elder sphinx can create a magical symbol as though casting a heightened [_glyph of warding_](/Spells.aspx?ID=138). The sphinx usually shapes it to the form of a riddle and sets the password to the answer. A creature that doesn't speak the password must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or be affected by one of the following spells, chosen by the sphinx when creating the symbol: [_visions of danger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=358) (7th), [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=301) (6th), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=327) (5th), [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (4th), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) (3rd), [_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) (3rd), or [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) (3rd). The sphinx learns the identity of any creature that answers the riddle and tends to be friendly to them
Ancient Guardians Elder sphinxes were revered as semi-divine by ancient cultures, hailed as wise prophets sent by the gods and trusted guardians of temple and necropolis alike, especially the burial sites of high kings and their dynastic heirs. Those in need of impartial rulings and divine inspiration make pilgrimage to ruins of ancient civilizations seeking the counsel of an elder sphinx.
name: Elder Sphinx

Spirit guides of cunning are often seen as the weakest of the spirit guides, but are also the most numerous and proactive, guiding families to safe paths or leaving food and water for warriors. Cunning guides often take the form of foxes. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Cunning Fox](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1319) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 252 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +5 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +8 **Will** +7 **HP** 14 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision **Resistances** all damage 2 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** horn +6 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d8+1 force **Melee** jaws +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d4+1 force **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **1st** [Detect Poison](/Spells.aspx?ID=67), [Purify Food and Drink](/Spells.aspx?ID=241) - **3rd** [Wanderer's Guide](/Spells.aspx?ID=368) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Bond with Mortal** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The spirit guide forms a bond with a mortal creature. While the bond exists, the spirit guide increases their current and maximum Hit Points by 10, gains a +2 status bonus to their attack and damage rolls, and can communicate telepathically with the bonded mortal as long as the two beings are on the same plane. The spirit guide can only be bonded with one mortal at a time, and they can take this action again to end the bond or to form a new bond (which also ends the old bond). The bond also ends if the spirit guide or the mortal dies.
This bond strengthens the spirit guide's connection to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). While bonded, the spirit guide loses the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) and [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) traits, loses their immunities and resistances, and changes their Strikes to deal the appropriate physical damage (typically piercing or slashing) instead of force damage. **Bonded Strike** **Requirements** The spirit guide is currently Bonded with a Mortal; **Effect** The spirit guide makes a jaws Strike. If this attack hits, the bonded mortal can spend their reaction to Strike the same target.
name: Cunning Fox

Born from spirits possessing the qualities of strength, endurance, and ferocity, these spirit guides act as martial guardians for mortals under their care. Most have a burning hatred for [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30), and other [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) who prey on mortal weaknesses. However, they also display care and consideration around mortal children, who in turn seem to instinctively recognize the beings as protectors. Strength takes many forms, some evident in their power—like the feathered bear—and some unassuming. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Feathered Bear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1320) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 253 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +16 **Will** +18 **HP** 160 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Guardian's Aegis** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 30 feet. All allies within 30 feet of the feathered bear gain a +1 status bonus to saves against [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) effects. The bonus increases to +2 if the effect originated from a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) or other chaotic evil [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70).
**Avenging Claws** **Trigger** A creature within 10 feet damages the feathered bear's ally with a melee attack; **Effect** The feathered bear immediately Steps toward the triggering attacker and makes a claws Strike. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d10+9 force **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6+9 force plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 27 - **3rd** [Barkskin](/Spells.aspx?ID=20), [Endure Elements](/Spells.aspx?ID=99), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=167), [Longstrider](/Spells.aspx?ID=175) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Bond with Mortal** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The spirit guide forms a bond with a mortal creature. While the bond exists, the spirit guide increases their current and maximum Hit Points by 20, gains a +2 status bonus to their attack and damage rolls, and can communicate telepathically with the bonded mortal as long as the two beings are on the same plane. The spirit guide can only be bonded with one mortal at a time, and they can take this action again to end the bond or to form a new bond (which also ends the old bond). The bond also ends if the spirit guide or the mortal dies.
This bond strengthens the spirit guide's connection to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). While bonded, the spirit guide loses the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) and [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) traits, loses their immunities and resistances, and changes their Strikes to deal the appropriate physical damage (typically piercing or slashing) instead of force damage. **Feathered Charge** The feathered bear Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. During the Stride, the feathered bear ignores difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain, and they can move across air as easily as solid ground. If the feathered bear doesn't end their movement on solid ground, they fall as soon as the Strike is completed. **Bonded Strike** **Requirements** The spirit guide is currently Bonded with a Mortal; **Effect** The spirit guide makes a jaws Strike. If this attack hits, the bonded mortal can spend their reaction to Strike the same target. **Mauler** The feathered bear gains a +4 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against creatures they have [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20).
name: Feathered Bear

markdown:
Nykteras, or bat sprites, are gentle sprites known for their affectionate nature and incredible hospitality. Nykteras share features with bats, but the type of bat varies by location, as nykteras tend to form small colonies in locations with bats similar to their own nature. A nyktera's sense of hospitality is such that they're willing to share with any who need it, even bitter foes. However, violating a nyktera's hospitality is one of their greatest taboos, inviting rare anger and retribution from the nyktera who was wronged, as well as their large extended family. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Nyktera](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1321) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 254 **Perception** +6; (8 to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) creatures using hearing) [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); speak with bats **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +5, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5 **Str** -3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Speak with Bats** A nyktera can communicate with bats and use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on bats and [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) things from bats.
**Wrath of Spurned Hospitality** A nyktera whose hospitality is betrayed becomes enraged at the violation of a fundamental aspect of their fey nature. They must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or begin involuntarily attacking the traitor. At the end of each of their turns, if they choose, they can attempt another Will save to end the effect; otherwise, the effect lasts until the traitor is subdued or leaves the nyktera's presence. As long as their righteous fury lasts, the nyktera gains a +2 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for their fist Strikes. --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +2 **Ref** +7 **Will** +6 **HP** 10 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 2 --- **Speed** 10 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d6–3 bludgeoning damage **Ranged** ultrasonic pulse +7 ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d4 sonic **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132) - **1st** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148)

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Melixie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1322)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Melixies are hyperactive insect sprites with legendary sweet tooths, capable of drinking down honey, nectar, sugar, and other sweets in alarming quantities. Melixies take the insectile wings common among winged fey and up the ante with antennae and additional insect features, typically shared with bees but sometimes with butterflies, moths, ladybugs, or other beetles. Melixies live in a pattern of rapid consumption of sweets, incredible productivity in tasks of all sorts, and then a crash to sleep it all off and start again. Melixies in their productive state even engage happily in large-scale repetitive tasks that most sprites find dreadfully dull. In their spare time, melixies love to dance, and some of them learn a dancelike sign language called Melisylvan, allowing them to communicate a full range of information through movement. 

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 14

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

**Speak with Arthropods** A melixie can communicate with arthropods, such as insects, spiders, scorpions, crabs, and similar invertebrate animals. They can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on arthropods and [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) things from arthropods.

</column>

</column>

stinger +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d6–1 bludgeoning damage

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sting shot +8 ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6 poison

- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132)
- **1st**
[Ant Haul](/Spells.aspx?ID=13)

**Sugar Rush** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The melixie consumes a large quantity of sugar, honey, nectar, or similar sweets and goes into a frenzied state. The melixie immediately Strides or Flies. For the next 1d4+1 rounds, the melixie is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32), and can use the additional action only to Stride or Fly. As soon as the rush ends, the melixie is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Minature Desserts</title>

While most melixies gobble up any sweets they find, those who can resist eating the raw ingredients for long enough learn to use them create incredibly small desserts in a startling variety of styles and flavors. Some members of larger ancestries even enjoy the way that a tray of melixie desserts allows them to experience so many different flavors before becoming full.
</aside>

name: Melixie

The mischievous dragon sprites known as draxies have dueled their [pixie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=391) cousins for the title of ultimate prankster for centuries. Possessed of draconic features and brilliant colorations, draxies descend from a mighty [faerie dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=205), reincarnated into multiple draxies after her death in the mercurial fashion of the fey realm. Draxies exercise patience and planning to create the perfect pranks, spending months, or even years, on their efforts. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Draxie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1323) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 255 **Perception** +8; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) (touch) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** -1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +11 **Will** +8 **HP** 45 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5 --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** jaws +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d8+3 bludgeoning damage **Ranged** euphoric spark +7 ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d4+3 mental **Primal Innate Spells** DC 20 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) - **1st** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) (×3) - **2nd** [Faerie Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=107), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) **Breath Weapon** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The draxie breathes pixie dust in a 15-foot cone, with a random effect determined each time they use their Breath Weapon. Each creature in the area must succeed at a DC 17 Will save or be affected. Roll 1d4 to determine the effect. The draxie can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.
**1.** The target takes the effects of the [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) spell.
**2.** The target loses its last 5 minutes of memory.
**3.** The target takes the effects of a [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) spell.
**4.** The target becomes [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) from euphoria.
Elucera One exception to the flighty nature of draxies is the elucrea, a lifelong bond formed between a draxie and a creature or creatures the draxie is particularly fond of, typically those with a good sense of humor. According to draxie legend, a little piece of a draxie's spirit still remembers being united together as the ancient [faerie dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=205) Elucredassa, and that causes draxies to yearn for such connections with others.
name: Draxie
