markdown:
In the great fighting tournaments across Tian Xia, a competitor will occasionally perish in the heat of battle. Sometimes their lust for battle, desire to win, or enduring competitive spirit lingers, often resulting in a haunting. When this happens, it's up to a ghost eater to defeat the lingering soul to send it to the afterlife. Ten years ago, during the previous Ruby Phoenix Tournament, Yong was a boy of 13 years working as a water carrier, though he dreamed of one day fighting in the tournament and making a name for himself, especially to set him apart from his many accomplished human, orc, and half-orc cousins. Yong imagined showing the world his skill, determination, and fierce grasp on life and excelling at something all his own. That very grasp saved his life when an errant undead specter disrupted a match, tearing through the ringside where Yong was carrying water. Though he was gravely injured in the incident, Yong was brought into the care of the tournament's resident ghost eater and, from her, learned how to fight the corruption of lingering souls. Now, Yong is one of the tournament's most renown ghost eaters, tasked with cleansing any haunts, ghosts, or spiritual phenomena that remain after a particularly grueling battle. Though he sometimes shudders at the memory of that terrifying day ten years ago, through sheer determination, he carries on, finding that in guiding these souls to rest, he's found not just a way to purge the ghosts of his own past, but a rare talent as a spirit tender, one all his own. Plot HooksGame Masters can use the following plot hooks to integrate Zhang Yong into their ongoing Pathfinder campaigns.
  • The players' competitors could be having trouble with a particularly powerful haunt or ghost disrupting a practice space and wish to seek Yong's aid to purge it.
  • The competitors hear of Yong's childhood trauma, and how those memories cause him distress today. They may wish to help the young man face those fears and move past the pain.
  • The ghost of Yong's former master has risen for this decade's Ruby Phoenix Tournament, thinking she is still the head ghost eater. Yong is having trouble facing his old master alone and seeks the players' competitors for guidance and assistance sending his old master safely to Pharasma's court.
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Zhang Yong](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1391) **Source** [No-Prep Character: Zhang Yong](/Sources.aspx?ID=84) **Perception** +23; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +19, [Haunt Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +23 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +2 **Items** _+2 [axiomatic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=291) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_, [_bracers of armor I_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=416), [greater ghost charge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=687) (2), rare incense --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +29 **Will** +22 **HP** 251 **Grit Teeth** ([ <%TRAITS%60%%> emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) <%END>, [ <%TRAITS%106%%> mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) <%END>, [pain](/Domains.aspx?ID=24)) **Trigger** Yong fails or critically fails a saving throw against an [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) or [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect; **Effect** Yong succeeds on the saving throw instead and takes 2d6 mental damage. If he critically failed on the triggering saving throw, he also takes 2d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and cannot use this ability again while taking persistent mental damage. --- **Speed** 45 **Melee** soul strike +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 2d8+14 bludgeoning (plus 1d6 lawful against chaotic targets) **Ranged** greater ghost charge +23 ([range 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 3d8 positive plus 3 positive [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage **Disrupting Strikes** Yong calls forth positive energy from within to help him destroy undead. His weapons and unarmed Strikes gain the effects of a [_greater disrupting_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=294) property rune until the start of his next turn (DC 31). **Reach Beyond** Yong has learned to move his spiritual energy past himself to attack spirits where others can't. He can make melee Strikes against [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures that are inside solid objects as long as the object is within his reach. An incorporeal creature inside of a solid object is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), requiring him to succeed at a DC 11 flat check when he targets it. He can spend an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to focus on a creature inside of a solid object. If he does, the next melee Strike he makes this turn requires him to succeed at only a DC 6 flat check to affect the creature. **Soul Dance Stance** Yong begins a stance that aligns him with the River of Souls, allowing him to make soul strike attacks that seem almost incorporeal. These deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage; are in the [brawling group](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4); and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits. Soul strikes treat cover against living and incorporeal undead targets as degree less severe (greater cover becomes standard cover, standard cover becomes lesser cover, and lesser cover is ignored).
name: Zhang Yong

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Most young anugobus undertake not just one, but many apprenticeships in order to learn advanced techniques in their trade of choice. The apprentice depicted here specializes in construction and engineering, but an anugobu is just as likely to take up cooking, hunting, tailoring, or really anything that piques their interest.

**Planar Workshop**   An anugobu is intrinsically connected to small, pocket dimension that they typically refer to as their workshop. The workshop is an extradimensional 10-foot cube. The workshop holds an anugobu's collected tools and materials. An anugobu can enter or leave their planar workshop as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) and [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) traits. The anugobu gains resistance 5 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) and concealment while within the workshop. The workshop is only visible while the anugobu is inside it.

**Stone Step**   An anugobu can effortlessly stick to any worked or manufactured surface, allowing them to walk up walls and stand on ceilings.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Anugobu Wondercrafter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1393)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Once an anugobu apprentice sets out on their own, they travel the world working on small projects here and there to maintain their skills. But an anugobu does not earn the honorable title of wondercrafter until they find their own wonder. Once they discover and finish such a wonder, the anugobu is considered fully mature. As a result of their station and experience, such anugobus possess skills and magic that far surpass most of their kind.

markdown:
Berberokas are giant humanoids who live among forests and swamps, where they use their ability to swallow massive amounts of water to drain small ponds and lakes. Creatures who visit their favorite watering hole and find only an empty mud basin become prey for the berberoka, who hides in the brush and overwhelm their prey with a massive torrent of regurgitated water. A berberoka's mottled backside—which resembles a bundle of local plants, small trees, and large rocks—grants them natural camouflage that allows them to hide in plain sight. Berberokas tend to disguise themselves as rock formations in the center of a dried-up waterbed while they lie in wait for passersby. In the tropical regions where berberokas are most common, locals know to give empty ponds a wide berth, regardless of the enticing fish flopping about. Hungry travelers, on the other hand, might see such bounty as a blessing from the gods, only to be swept up in the berberoka's deadly deluge. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Berberoka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1394) **Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 82 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Deep Breath** A berberoka can hold their breath for 2 hours. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +23 **Will** +24 **HP** 310 **Weaknesses** fear of crabs **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Fear of Crabs** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) If a berberoka sees a [crab](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=129) or crab-like creature, the berberoka must attempt a DC 33 Will save. They then becomes immune to the sight of that creature for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The berberoka is unaffected.
**Success** The berberoka becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The berberoka gains the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) condition for 1 round and is frightened 4. --- **Speed** 30 feet, swim 30 feet; 15 feet while waterlogged **Melee** fist +31 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+16 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** 2d12+12 bludgeoning, DC 33 **Consume Lake** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The berberoka drinks a prolific amount from an adjacent water source. If the water source is equal to or greater in volume than themself, the berberoka consumes up to 1,500 gallons of water per minute and becomes waterlogged. They can release water at the same rate. While waterlogged, the berberoka can use Spray Water, their size grows to Gargantuan, and their Speed is reduced to 15 feet. **Spray Water** **Requirements** The berberoka is waterlogged; **Effect** The berberoka sprays a blast of water in a 60-foot line. All creatures in the line take 6d8 bludgeoning damage (DC 35 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a failed save, a creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) back 5 feet (10 feet on a critically failed save).
Kabourophobia [Berberokas](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1394) share an unusually intense fear of crabs, so many folktales suggest fishers bring a crab with them should they suspect a berberoka nearby. If no crab is available, clicking one's tongue or snapping one's fingers might suffice.
name: Berberoka

<title level="1" pfs="">[Dromornis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1395)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dromornises are large, flightless, carnivorous birds that travel in packs to ambush prey. Their short, stubby wings may give them a comical appearance, but anyone who's been on the receiving end of the beast's powerful beak finds the experience less than amusing. Dromornis use their beaks to easily tear flesh and sinew from prey's bones, quickly dispatching sizable mammals like deer, tapirs, and primates. Particularly hungry or aggressive dromornises have even been known to attack humanoids. The bird's strong legs sport sharp talons that, while secondary in combat to its beak, can nonetheless trample and shred smaller creatures like rodents and amphibians caught underfoot.

 While a lone dromornis is dangerous enough, a pack of dromornises can take down a den of bears or village of humanoids. A dromornis pack typically consists of three or four adults and up to five juveniles, all of whom work in tandem to overpower larger prey. Packs lie in wait for something like an anaconda, boar, buffalo or, in cases of desperation, a dinosaur to emerge, then move as one for a surprise strike. Dromornises aren't particularly intelligent, but they have been known to use an ingenious (if hazardous) decoy tactic, luring in massive prey by putting one of their own fledglings at risk.

 Dromornises most often live in tropical jungles far from the reaches of civilization, though they sometimes make their homes in mountainside caves or along rocky coastlines. While most onlookers would be hard-pressed to tell one dromornis from another, dromornises maintain tight familial structures based on pheromones and scent glands, so it's obvious when a straggler from another family is trying to encroach on a pack's den. Such disputes over breeding grounds are common; a particularly prized dromornis den might change hands several times in the course of a year. Fights between dromornis packs are chaotic and swift, marked with the sounds of terrifying screeches and sprays of blood.

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Hunting Dromornises</title>

[Dromornis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1395) hides and beaks garner a relatively high asking price on markets. However, finding a dromornis is easier said than done, as they tend to claim dens in the remote parts of a jungle. Even once a hunter has found a dromornis den, they then have to contend with several of the birds at once, since dromornises almost never lair alone. Facing off against a pack is a deadly prospect, but might be well worth the trouble for an enormous payoff.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Hantu Belian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1397)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The hantu belian represents the spiritual essence of a tiger and typically manifests as a ghostly bird perched on a tiger's back. Hantu belians commit acts of great destruction as well as restoration. They sometimes possess humanoids to cause great discord and devastation, though their intentions are typically borne of amusement or boredom rather than malice.

Caustic monitors are enormous lizards native to eastern Minata known and named for the corrosive enzyme in their mucus, saliva, and other bodily fluids. These corrosive fluids originally developed as a means of protection from large insects such as giant mosquitos. The thick layer of mucus that naturally coats the monitors' scaly skin makes it difficult for most other creatures to get too close, let alone prey upon the lizard. Caustic monitors also use their corrosive biological features to their hunting advantage, particularly by expelling the acidic contents of their stomach at prey. Even when not making use of their acid, caustic monitors can be deadly predators: They grab onto smaller creatures using their powerful forelimb muscles and are remarkably agile, considering their size and weight. Most caustic monitors live among rocky terrain such as pebble beaches, natural caves, and along sandy river shoals. A caustic monitor's mucus corrodes only flesh, so its den is safe from damage, but corroded plant life near a cave is a telltale sign that a caustic monitor lives nearby. Hardy adventurers and fortune seekers must weigh the potential benefits of hunting this beast—as its acid is highly prized among alchemists—against the obvious risks. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Caustic Monitor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1398) **Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 86 **Perception** +22; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** -4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +23 **Will** +20 **HP** 235 **Corrosive Flesh** A creature that touches the caustic monitor takes 2d8 acid damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save).
**Gnashing Grip** **Trigger** A creature grabbed by the caustic monitor's jaws fails a check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79); **Effect** The caustic monitor's jaws deal 2d8 piercing damage and the triggering creature is exposed to caustic monitor venom. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing plus 2d8 acid, [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27), and caustic monitor venom **Melee** talon +27, **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Caustic Spray** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3)) The caustic monitor spits a spray of corrosive bile that deals 5d8 acid damage in a 20-foot cone (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). It can't use Caustic Spray again for 1d4 rounds. **Lurching Charge** The caustic monitor Strides twice, then makes a jaws Strike. If the monitor moved at least 20 feet away from its starting position, it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to this attack roll. **Caustic Monitor Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round)
Monitor Acid Many alchemists prize the corrosive enzymes of a caustic monitor to enhance their own acidic concoctions and pay hunters handsomely for freshly gutted specimens. Harvesting a caustic monitor's acid requires a successful [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) or [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22) check against a very hard DC of the caustic monitor's level (DC 36 for most caustic monitors). On a success, the gutter gathers 2 doses of caustic monitor acid, or 4 doses on a critical success. Applying a dose of caustic monitor acid to an [acid flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=74) increases the [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) the flask deals on a hit by one damage die (from 1d6 to 2d6, for example). The monitor acid expires after 1 week.
name: Caustic Monitor

<title level="1" pfs="">[Manananggal](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1399)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Manananggals are wicked monsters that live as humanoids during the day but feed upon them at night. To hide their depravities, these beings disguise themselves as everyday people—typically recluses or eccentrics— and use their proximity to a community to pick out their next targets before striking under cover of night. Manananggals use their long, hollow tongues to drain victims' blood from vital organs such as the liver, and the resulting odd drainage marks make it difficult to determine the exact nature of a victim's death. This typically leads to confusion, terror, and mistrust among communities, which most manananggals savor. While they fly, manananggals make a distinctive ticking sound, leading some to refer to the creatures as “tik-tiks,” and badly affected communities descend into fearful chaos at the first reports of this telltale noise.

 Manananggals share their cannibalistic, body-separating traits with other types of tanngals, such as [penanggalans](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1261), though a number of characteristics set them apart. For instance, whereas penanggalans relish vinegar and even bathe in the substance, manananggals abhor the merest scent of it.

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 26
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58)
- **1st**

**Blood Drain** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The manananggal has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The manananggal drives its tongue into the grabbed creature to drink its blood. This deals 4d6 damage, and the manananggal gains temporary Hit Points equal to the damage dealt. A creature whose blood is drained by a manananggal is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) until it receives healing (of any kind or amount).

**Separated Body**   A manananggal typically resembles a living [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91). It doesn't appear as an aberration at first glance and receives a +4 status bonus to its [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC when [Impersonating](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) a humanoid. At any time but especially at night, a manananggal can, as a three-action activity with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait, rip its upper torso from its bottom half, leaving the lower torso limp and helpless but allowing the upper torso to fly free. A manananggal can separate its body at any time, but it risks further damage if it does so during the day. The manananggal's upper and lower halves share the same pool of HP. If its lower half is damaged, the manananggal is immediately aware of the attack. Each minute a manananggal remains separated after sunrise and before sunset, it takes 2d6 points of damage until it reattaches its upper and lower torsos or is destroyed, whichever comes first.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Grim Blades</title>

Minatan folktales have it that a forgotten demigod created the first manananggals by bisecting the torso of a human with a divine knife. The demigod then cut into the human's shoulders and drove a pair of bat wings into their back, birthing the first manananggal. The divine power of the demigod's knife still resonates in every manananggal and makes each of the aberrations susceptible to cuts from similar weapons. Communities dealing with a manananggal's terror train with light blades and other slashing weapons to cut the monsters from the sky.
</aside>
name: Manananggal

<title level="1" pfs="">[Muckish Creep](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1400)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Legends say that the first muckish creeps were born at the site of a mass grave where the souls of murdered prisoners coalesced into the surrounding soil. Another myth tells that muckish creeps were elementals so vile that their kin banished them from the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5). Regardless of their true origins, muckish creeps are wicked creatures that resemble mud given a humanoid shape. The mud is no ordinary mixture of earth and water, however, for muckish creeps feed on the blood of the living, and they use this blood to keep their own forms moist and malleable.

 Muckish creeps are particularly tenacious and single-minded when it comes to choosing prey. Once a muckish creep selects its next victim, there is little one can do to stop it, since it can seep through even the smallest cracks between doors, walls, and windows. A muckish creep typically waits until its target falls asleep before pressing itself into the creature's home, intent to kill. For all its tenacity during the hunt, the muckish creep is pragmatic when it comes to the actual kill, using its body to fill the victim's airways with mud and suffocate them.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/MuckishCreep.png" />
</column>

</row>

**Elude Grasp** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature succeeds at an attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) the muckish creep; **Effect** The muckish creep attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +18,
**Damage** 2d10+10 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mud ball +18 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning plus blinding mud

**Blinding Mud**   When a muckish creep hits a creature with a mud ball Strike, that creature becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) (or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) on a critical hit). The creature can use an Interact action to wipe away the mud and remove the dazzled or blinded condition.

**Compression**   A muckish creep can move through a gap at least 6 inches wide without [Squeezing](/Actions.aspx?ID=31) and can Squeeze through any gap at least 1 inch wide.

**Mud Trail**   When a muckish creep Strides or Steps, it leaves behind a slimy mud trail that coats the traversed area. All traversed squares are covered in [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) (DC 26) until the start of the muckish creep's next turn.

**Overwhelm** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) **Requirements** The muckish creep has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The muckish creep fills the grabbed creature's mouth and nose with mud. The creature begins to [suffocate](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) unless it succeeds at a DC 23 Fortitude save. The suffocating creature or any adjacent creatures can clear the suffocating creature's airway with an Interact action.

**Slippery Body**   A muckish creep's body allows it to slip away from most restraints. It gains a +4 status bonus to checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). On a success, it gets a critical success instead. On a critical failure, it gets a failure instead.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Cursed Mud</title>

When a muckish creep's bloody mud is used as mortar in building construction, it sometimes creates a new muckish creep fused with the structure. Such a creature can move the entire building as if it were part of its own body. In the jungles and rainforests where muckish creeps are common, people often call upon clerics to bless their building materials, including the mud, in order to avoid unintentionally creating such a being.
</aside>
name: Muckish Creep

<title level="1" pfs="">[Sigbin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1401)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Resembling hornless goats with extremely long tails, sigbins live in forests and prey on inhabitants of nearby humanoid villages. Sigbins drink the blood of their victims by biting into their shadows, rather than their flesh. This process typically leaves the victim so fatigued that they must lie down to rest, at which point the sigbin finishes the job by sinking their jaws into the person's chest and consuming their heart.

 Due to their nauseating smell, many sigbins hide among livestock and in other overwhelmingly odorous places. Sigbins have the ability to hide in plain sight simply by standing stock still, but this power works only against adults and elders; for whatever reason, children and young animals can see the invisible sigbins. Many believe this is because children are unsullied by the cruel ways of the world, and so their eyes are still innocent enough to spot the devious beasts. Unfortunately, most adults tend to ignore children's cries about the monsters, chalking up such claims to overactive imaginations or attention-seeking behavior, inadvertently allowing sigbins to continue their hunts uninterrupted.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

While within the aura, the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is immune to all sigbins' stenches for 1 minute.

**Shadow Drain** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The sigbin is [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41) by its target; **Effect** The sigbin makes a fangs Strike (unlike other Strikes, this does not end its invisibility if the sigbin is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26)). On a hit, the target takes [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) damage from the Strike and becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 2 on a critical hit). The sigbin gains 15 temporary Hit Points that remain for 1 hour.

**Unassuming Cloak**  ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92)) A sigbin becomes almost imperceptible if it stands completely still. If the sigbin does not move or attack during its turn, it becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26). It remains invisible until it moves or takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300). Children and young creatures, like animal cubs, can see the sigbin normally, even while it's invisible.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Sigbinans</title>

Rumors hold that wicked individuals can capture [sigbins](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1401) and use them for nefarious purposes. These monster hunters are known as sigbinans. Capturing a sigbin is a dangerous process that requires using a living person as bait and a large magical jar as the trap. Once they've captured a sigbin, the sigbinan can deploy their monster to terrorize others. In the jungles of Vudra, sigbinans have been known to capture sigbins to create magical amulets that, when donned, grant the wearer the sigbin's ability to drink blood through a person's shadow.
</aside>
name: Sigbin

Angoyangs resemble white cats with black stripes like those of a tiger. They have beautifully silken fur, piercing blue eyes, and long fluffy tails. Yet, far from the picturesque feline companions they appear to be, angoyangs are actually terrifying beasts capable of peeling back the fleshy layers of their face to reveal a mass of long, toothed tentacles. Although most cats instinctively avoid areas where angoyangs roam, the unlucky felines who do encounter this creature are invariably entangled in the monster's toothy tentacles and devoured. In cities and towns where angoyangs reside, cat owners may find that their beloved pets have gone missing only to soon have a fluffy black-and-white cat coincidentally appear at their doorstep, looking up at them with wide eyes and mewing adorably. Angoyangs almost never attack their caretakers, who they see as loyal servants that feed, clean up after, and pamper them, and the creatures protect the people in their household from any would-be intruders. However, a caretaker who mistreats or neglects their angoyang ward is often met with a grisly fate. More than one coroner or gravedigger has inadvertently inherited an angoyang after being assigned to clean up the mutilated remains of a previous “owner.” **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Angoyang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1402) **Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 78 **Perception** +25; dander scent (1 mile, imprecise), [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +25 **Str** +3 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 **I Am A Cat** The angoyang has an automatic result of 47 to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to appear as a cat.
**Dander Scent** The angoyang can imprecisely sense the location of any creature within 1 mile that has previously come in contact with the angoyang's fur. --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +29 **Will** +25 **HP** 250 --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d10+9 slashing **Melee** tentacles +27 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+9 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Silent Yowl** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The angoyang opens its mouth and drops its innocent disguise, releasing its monstrous tentacles and letting out a blood-curdling scream directed at a single target that it can see to deal 8d10 mental damage. Only the target of this ability can hear this scream. The target must succeed at a DC 30 Will save as the sound tears through their mind.
**Critical Success** The target takes no damage and is immune to Silent Yowl for 24 hours.
**Success** The target takes half damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and is frightened 3. **Slow Blink** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The angoyang lets out a small meow and slowly blinks twice at a single creature. The target must attempt a DC 31 Will save. On a critical success, the target is unaffected and immune to Slow Blink for 24 hours. Otherwise, the creature is overcome with the urge to pet the angoyang's silken fur, becoming [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14). If the angoyang attacks the creature, the creature can attempt a new save at the beginning of their next turn.
Lucky Cat? Due to their appearance as cats and their monstrous intellect, angoyangs are skilled at getting around unnoticed and often collect useful trinkets and information for their own mysterious purposes. Sometimes an angoyang will leave their stolen valuables inside the homes of “lucky” individuals. Such individuals may find small valuables, coins, or notes with trade secrets in their shoes and pockets and believe them to be a divine blessing. However, the angoyang's motives are likely far from benign.
name: Angoyang

Hungry and resentful, the malevolent kun is a mythological fish monster that dwells in the remotest, coldest depths of the ocean. This massively powerful creatures could easily rule a subaquatic domain if they cared to, but kuns are also famous for their total self-absorption and complete ambivalence toward other forms of life. A kun remains as far away from other creatures as they can, subsisting on only their own icy hatred. They can consume and digest prey, but such feedings are incidental. Most kuns remain ignorant and uncaring, keeping themselves in the dark, disregarding any signs of life around them except to occasionally lash out with a spiteful attack. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Kun](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1403) **Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 79 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [light blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=41) 60 feet **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +7 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +24 **Will** +23 **HP** 230 **Resistances** cold 10 **Weaknesses** good 10 **Spite** **Trigger** A creature within reach of the kun's tail uses an action to attack or heal another creature (including the kun); **Effect** The kun bats the triggering creature away with their tail, making a tail Strike against the creature. On a hit, the action is disrupted and the creature is pushed 10 feet. --- **Speed** swim 40 feet **Melee** jaws +30 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +28 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 3d12+14 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 32 - **7th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) (at will), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will), [Wall of Ice](/Spells.aspx?ID=364) **Funnel** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) The kun inhales violently, sucking in heat and all creatures in the water in a 60-foot cone. Creatures in the area must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or take 10d8 cold damage. The kun can't Funnel again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is pulled 30 feet toward the kun.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is pulled 60 feet toward the kun. **Heart of Darkness** Whenever the kun makes an attack from within an area of darkness, their attack deals an additional 2d6 evil damage. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge, 3d12+7 bludgeoning, Rupture 30
Change of Heart The kun hunts at the bottom of the cold, dark sea. But if shone the light of the sun, a kun begins to understand that it dwells within a different, bigger world. When flocks of birds or schools of fish cast their dappled shadows upon a kun, they might grow weary of their solitude or become curious about the realm above. In extremely rare circumstances and for their own mysterious reasons, a kun might sacrifice some of their power to undergo a permanent, one-time transformation. In a final dramatic act, the kun flings themself up and out of the ocean, allowing the sun to fully wash over them. In the process, the kun transforms from the hateful fish into a noble and curious bird called a [peng](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1406).
name: Kun

Oni who assume the form of [shadow giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=681) are known as shadow yais. Many shadow yais crave the pleasures of music and go to great lengths to secure bardic or musically inclined supplicants. Smaller beings who refuse to submit to a shadow yai are subject to painful, drawn out, and strangely beautiful deaths, for a shadow yai has a special way of evoking melodic cries and screams from their victims: a veritable symphony of pain. Individuals of particular musical skill who accompany a shadow yai can find the experience extremely lucrative and enriching—as long as they don't run afoul of their liege. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 35 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Shadow Yai](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1404) **Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 80 **Perception** +28; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +31, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +33, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +30 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_ --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +28 **Will** +26 **HP** 290 , regeneration 15 (deactivated by acid or fire) **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet; Shadow Step **Melee** _glaive_ +32 ([deadly 2d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus 2d6 sonic **Melee** fist +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus shadow touch **Ranged** sonic missile +30 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d10+11 sonic plus [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round **Primal Innate Spells** DC 37 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only) - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104) (at will) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (×3), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) - **5th** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) (×3), [Shadow Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=275) - **8th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The shadow yai takes on the appearance of a [shadow giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=681). This doesn't change its Speed or Strike attack and damage. **Shadow Step** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Requirements** The shadow yai is in an area of dim light or darkness or is standing in the shadow of any Large or larger object; **Effect** The shadow yai instantly teleports to another location that's in dim light or darkness or to a space that's in the shadow of any Large or larger object within 60 feet. **Shadow Touch** A shadow yai's touch harms their target's spiritual essence in addition to its body. A creature hit by the shadow yai's fist Strike must attempt a DC 36 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d6 negative damage.
**Failure** The creature becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and takes 3d6 negative damage. As long as the creature is drained as a result of this ability, it loses the ability to speak above a whisper. When the creature attempts to Cast a Spell with a verbal component, it must succeed at a flat check or the spell is disrupted. The DC of this flat check is equal to 5 plus the creature's drained value (for example, DC 7 if the creature is drained 2).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature becomes drained 2 and takes 6d6 negative damage.
Shadow Competitions When shadow yais congregate they sometimes hold musical competitions between their servants. Wagers often include the performers themselves, so a servant might attend to many shadow yais over their life. Yais that feel cheated in these games redouble their recruitment in surrounding areas for an upper hand in the next contest.
name: Shadow Yai

Wearing the visage of [taiga giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=680), taiga yais generally travel in a solitary fashion, always yearning for new experiences and destinations. They are zealous in their love of nature in its purest form and gleefully destroy any established civilizations that they encounter before continuing onwards. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Taiga Yai](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1405) **Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 81 **Perception** +30; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +30, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +32 **Str** +6 **Dex** +8 **Con** +8 **Int** +5 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77)_ (20 arrows) --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +29 **Will** +30 +1 status on all saves vs. magic **HP** 270 , regeneration 15 (deactivated by fire) **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Botanic Interruption** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** The taiga yai is standing in natural foliage or undergrowth and is targeted by a melee attack; **Effect** The taiga yai infuses their power within the plants around them, causing the plants to shoot up and create a protective barrier. The taiga yai gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack roll. If the attack misses, the attacker must attempt a DC 33 Reflex save. On a failed save, the attacker is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 round as the plants grab hold of them; on a critical failure, the attacker is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** fist +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+12 bludgeoning plus 2d6 acid **Ranged** acid missile +29 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 3d8+10 acid plus [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round **Ranged** _shortbow_ +31 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 34 - **2nd** [Entangle](/Spells.aspx?ID=103) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) - **6th** [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=331) (×2) - **7th** [Shifting Sand](/Spells.aspx?ID=586) (×2) - **8th** [Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=95) (×2), [Summon Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) - **Constant (8th)** [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The taiga yai takes on the appearance of a [taiga giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=680). This doesn't change their Speed or Strike attack and damage. **Return to Nature** ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The taiga yai commands underground plant roots to drag a creature within 60 feet into the earth. The target must succeed at a DC 35 Fortitude save or be [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1d4 rounds. After this time, the creature is buried; it is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), begins to [suffocate](/Rules.aspx?ID=468), and takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage each round they remain buried. To escape either while immobilized or grabbed, the creature must succeed at a DC 35 [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) check or an adjacent ally must succeed at a DC 33 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to pull them out.
Oni loners More so than other oni, taiga yais prefer seclusion or the company of evil beasts and wicked fey over that of other humanoids. They often befriend carnivorous animals, frequently partnering with apex predators such as bears and lions.
name: Taiga Yai

<title level="1" pfs="">[Peng](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1406)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Pengs are massive avian beasts who fly effortlessly among the highest clouds. Their bright plumage makes it difficult to see their shape against an azure sky, but with patience, an onlooker might notice movement too swift to be a natural cloud.

 Beautiful and graceful, pengs arise from the unlikeliest of origins: the cruel [kun](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1403), a hateful fish monster whose predilection for the crushing depths of the sea matches the peng's own love of the bright, open sky. After a kun sacrifices their power for a noble heart by bathing in the sun's rays, they transform into this majestic bird, which eschews violence unless they must fight to stay free. Pengs do not seek danger or thrills; they want only to make their long voyages and fly without disruption.

 A peng's calm and steady nature is so strong that its mere presence can quell storms and still winds. Mariners and pilots believe spotting a peng is a sign of good luck. When the sky is clear and blue and the weather gentle for an extended period, people attribute such fine conditions to the presence of an unseen peng.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 35

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Peng.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Peng](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1406)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="NG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 82

**Perception** +25

**Languages**
[Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +25

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +6

**Con** +5

**Int** +3

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +7
</row>

**Heaven's View** Clouds don't impair a peng's vision.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +24 

**Will** +23 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 200

</row>

**Resistances**
cold 10

**Weaknesses**
evil 10

**Calm Weather Aura** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 1 mile. The ambient weather around a peng is always lightly cloudy with a pleasant breeze, and the temperature is pleasantly warm for the season. A creature that attempts to alter the weather within this aura (such as with a [_control weather_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9) ritual or localized effect like [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143)) must succeed at a DC 33 Will save; otherwise the effect is disrupted. The peng can activate or deactivate this aura as a single action (this action has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 70 feet; windslice

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +22 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+10 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
talon +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 3d6+10 slashing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 31
- **5th**
[Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (at will), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=367) (at will)

**Windslice**   Pengs can move in any wind with easy grace. A peng ignores difficult terrain caused by wind and doesn't need to [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30) in high winds.

**Cloud Cover** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The peng exhales a thick cloud and envelops themself in the mist. The peng gains cover until the end of their next turn.

**Flap Wings** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The peng beats its wings to create a mighty burst of frosty air and push away foes. Each creature within 10 feet of the peng takes 3d6 bludgeoning and 3d6 cold damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that fail their save are [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) away 10 feet; creatures that critically fail are pushed away 20 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The peng can't Flap Wings again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Clear Direction</title>

Most pengs on Golarion are seen in the skies of southern Tian Xia. Pengs hardly ever roost or even stop to rest, and they are almost always seen headed in a southerly trajectory. According to pengs themselves, they have no destination: they are headed south, and even they don't know why. Some whimsical pengs claim that they are in pursuit of a dream they once had in a life they lived long before this one.
</aside>
name: Peng

<title level="1" pfs="">[Rivka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1407)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Rivkas are parasitic monsters that cling to the sides of kaiju. In their natural form, they resemble giant cockroaches with eight razor-like legs and two mouths—one between two vicious pincers plus a second, much larger mouth on their abdomen. With this secondary mouth they feed off the primordial energy of whichever kaiju they've latched onto.

 In the wake of a kaiju attack, a rivka detaches from its host and wades into the disaster zone to prey on the unsuspecting populace by presenting itself as an emissary of the kaiju and demanding tribute. According to this cruel charlatan, only sacrifices of blood and gold can appease their master—though of course the rivka absconds with these offerings before the gifts ever reach the kaiju (such tributes would mean little to the kaiju anyway). Particularly heinous rivkas search the rubble for dead victims to impersonate, magically adopting the visage of a beloved city elder or priest in order to stoke the citizens' paranoia.

 Drinking kaiju blood has a strange effect on a rivka's anatomy, enriching both its defensive and offensive capabilities with the primal energy of its host. Other creatures have attempted to drink kaiju blood and replicate this phenomenon, always to terrible effect. Clearly rivkas have coevolved with kaiju in order to metabolize their ancient blood.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 36

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Rivka.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Rivka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1407)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 83

**Perception** +21

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +7

**Con** +5

**Int** +0

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +8
</row>

**Kaiju Blood** Each rivka gains resistances and offensive powers by feasting on the primordial blood of a kaiju. The type of damage the rivka resists and the type of damage dealt by its Kaiju Ray depend on the kaiju it last fed upon. Some of the most typical rivkas include those that feed on **Cimurlian** (cold), **Kujiba** (negative), **Igroon** (acid), **Mogaru** (fire), or **Yorak** (electricity).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 

**Fort** +24 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +17 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 220

</row>

**Resistances**
one type 10 (see above)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 3d6+11 slashing

**Innate Primal Spells** DC 33
- **5th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) (×3)

**Eviscerate** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The rivka has not attacked yet this turn; **Effect** The rivka lashes out with four of its razor-like claws. It makes four Strikes: the first at its highest attack bonus, the next two with a –4 penalty to their attack rolls, and the last one with a –8 penalty. This ability counts as four Strikes for the purpose of its multiple attack penalty.

**Kaiju Ray** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The rivka opens its maw and projects a 60-foot line of primal energy that deals 14d6 damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The type of damage dealt by the ray depends on which kaiju the rivka has most recently fed from (see above). The rivka can't use Kaiju Ray again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Blending In</title>

While rivkas generally don't use the same illusory disguise more than once during their forays into civilization, some rivkas become eerily attached to one particular guise. Such a rivka uses the same disguise wherever it goes, at most changing clothes, and might develop a reputation or even cult following as a speaker for a particular kaiju. Such deceptions rarely end well for the cocky rivka when their treachery is inevitably discovered.
</aside>
name: Rivka

**Warped Fulu** --- **Speed** 30 feet; rigor mortis **Melee** claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d10+13 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Monk Focus Spells** 3 Focus Points, DC 34 - **8th** [Ki Rush](/Spells.aspx?ID=485), [Medusa's Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=739), [Wind Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=490) **Drain Qi** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When Draining Qi, the minister of tumult regains 19 HP. **Premonition of Death** **Trigger** The minister rolls initiative or their turn begins; **Effect** The minister is attuned to coming violence. The minister uses Stance of Death. **Stance of Death** ([Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=152), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The minister's qi-engorged form allows perfect body control at all times. They assume one of the following stances.

  • **Cloudless Void** The jiang-shi gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and their claw Strikes gain the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) trait.
  • **Sundered Veins** Creatures struck by the jiang-shi's claw Strikes take 1d10 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). On a critical hit, they are also [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13).

<title level="1" pfs="">[Canopy Elder](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1410)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In the densest and oldest jungles, arboreals can reach heights of 200 feet or more. Given thousands of undisturbed years, these massive creatures enter into a symbiotic relationship with a myriad of plants, fungi, and animals to achieve the esteemed status of canopy elder. They provide a safe home for the creatures they host and develop a variety of powers that help them defend their forest realms.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 44

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Arboreal_CanopyElder.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 19">[Canopy Elder](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1410)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 78

**Perception** +32; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 120 feet

**Languages**
[Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +35, [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +35, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +40, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +10

**Dex** +4

**Con** +9

**Int** +6

**Wis** +8

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 42 

**Fort** +36 

**Ref** +28 

**Will** +33 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 445

</row>

**Resistances**
bludgeoning 15, piercing 15

**Weaknesses**
[axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) vulnerability 15, fire 15

**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
branch +37 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d12+18 bludgeoning and [Improved Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=29) 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
root +37 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning plus [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
vine +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d6+18 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 38
- **9th**
[Nature's Enmity](/Spells.aspx?ID=205) (×4)
- **Constant (9th)**
[Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294)

**Fungal Web** <actions string="Single Action" />  The canopy elder launches a rapidly growing net of fungus from the mushrooms growing on its body at a single creature within 80 feet. The target must attempt a DC 39 Reflex save or become [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) by the fungal web ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC 39). While restrained, the creature takes 4d6 poison damage at the end of each of their turns.

**Pollen Cloud** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The canopy elder releases a dense cloud of pollen from the epiphytes living in its branches. The cloud emanates from the canopy elder in a 40-foot emanation. The cloud doesn't move with the canopy elder and remains in place for 1d6 rounds before dissipating. Creatures inside the cloud become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures within it. Non-[plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) and non-[fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=77) creatures within the cloud must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and on a critical failure the creature is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute. Regardless of the result, a creature is then temporarily immune to the sickening and blinding effects of the canopy elder's pollen for 24 hours.

**Vine Splint** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89)) The canopy elder grows a mesh of vines around its broken branches, repairing the damage and restoring 40 HP to itself. The canopy elder can't use Vine Splint again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Canopy Elder Allies</title>

Epiphytes, hallucinogenic mushrooms, carnivorous orchids, and stinging insects all call a canopy elder home, as might larger creatures such as primates, big cats, birds, bats, and snakes. Many of these residents will take great pains to defend their home from threats.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-8" />
name: Canopy Elder

markdown:
Bul-gaes are manifestations of the eclipsed sun and moon. They resemble large canines, though their coloration and features change as they exercise their powers over heat and cold. Depending on whether they have most recently unleashed their lunar or solar powers, a bul-gae resembles either a crimson hound wreathed in flames or a cerulean, frostbitten fox, respectively. Legends say that the first bul-gae attempted to steal the sun from the sky. As they bit down on the burning sphere, though, the bul-gae was badly burned. Having failed, they then tried to retrieve the moon, but when they touched the moon the bul-gae froze solid and fell to the ground. The descendants of that bul-gae forever hone their powers of flame and ice so that they may one day succeed where their ancestor failed. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Bul-Gae](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1411) **Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 79 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +7 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +22 **Will** +25 **HP** 255 **Resistances** cold 15, fire 15 **Eclipse Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 100 feet. Light and shadows morph strangely around the bul-gae. A creature attempting to create a [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) effect within the aura must attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the attempt fails, the effect is lost, and the DC to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) effects within the aura is increased by 1.
**Solar Flare** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** The bul-gae takes damage; **Effect** The bul-gae releases a solar flare in a 30-foot emanation. Each creature in the area takes 6d8 fire damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature also takes 2d10 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). After using this ability, the bul-gae's flames cool and they can't use Solar Flare again until after they have used Lunar Burst. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing plus 2d6 cold or fire and [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) **Lunar Burst** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The bul-gae releases a blast of otherworldly frost that deals 6d8 cold damage to each creature in a 10-foot emanation (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature also becomes [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). After using this ability, the bul-gae's flames heat up and they can't use Lunar Burst again until after they have used Solar Flare. **Shadow Leap** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Requirement** The bul-gae is in dim light or darkness; **Effect** The bul-gae dives into a shadow and reappears from a shadow within 40 feet. **Thermal Mastery** The bul-gae has total control over the powers of cold and fire. Each time it attacks with its jaws, it can choose to have the additional damage be either cold damage or fire damage.
Eclipsed Light Bul-gae bring with them the distorted light of an eclipse wherever they go. This distorted light favors blue hues and skews the spectrum of visible colors, giving the world an eerie, muted ambiance. Tales say that bul-gae serve as faithful companions to the ancient, shadowy master of the first bul-gae. These tales purport that the shadow master can see through the distorted light of all bul-gae and uses the hounds as a means to watch the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) for unknown purposes.
name: Bul-Gae

markdown:
Countless temples across Golarion, dedicated to deities both living and forgotten, have risen and fallen through the ages. Many find themselves buried under sand, snow, or vine never to be found. In such places, the spirits of the faithful can bind with their ruined temple to form a guardian construct, though with nothing left to guard, these spirits often become hollow, resentful, and filled with malice. These “desecrated guardians” lie in wait, sometimes for centuries, and destroy any adventurers who happen upon them. The most powerful desecrated guardians, such as the one presented here, incorporate the structures of multiple temples into their massive serpentine forms. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Desecrated Guardian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1412) **Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 80 **Perception** +30; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +9 **Int** -5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +31 **Will** +25 **HP** 360 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Weaknesses** good 15 **Desecration Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) 40 feet. Desecrated guardians exude a palpable malice that nauseates all but the most wicked passersby. Non-evil creatures that enter the aura must attempt a DC 37 Will save. On a failure, the creature becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). In addition, any non-evil creatures that attempt to Cast a Spell within the aura must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the spell is lost.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([deadly 3d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+22 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tail +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+20 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Avalanche** The desecrated guardian thrashes its body about, causing the nearby terrain to crumble and crash down on creatures in a 60-foot cone, dealing 12d10 bludgeoning damage (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). It can't use Avalanche again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiled Rockslide** If the desecrated guardian started its turn [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), it can Trample and remain hidden until after its attacks. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+18 bludgeoning, DC 39 **Rebuild** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The desecrated guardian draws in rubble from the surrounding area to rebuild its damaged body. It recovers 8d6 Hit Points. It can only use this ability if it is near suitable rock or rubble. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge, 3d8+20 bludgeoning, Rupture 45 **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Huge or smaller, tail, DC 37
Desecrated Temples Many of the temples that dot the peaks of the Wall of Heaven lay abandoned or in complete ruin. The magic in some of these places lives on, corrupted by time, decay, and the nightmarish extraplanar powers of Leng often found at Golarion's highest altitudes. There are dark tales of adventurers seeking refuge within a lone temple along the Wall of Heaven only to meet a terrible death at the hands of the temple's desecrated guardian. Although granting hospitality to strangers is a tenet of many Wall of Heaven temples, stories of monstrosities in disguise give some climbers pause as they approach a potential sanctuary.
name: Desecrated Guardian

markdown:
Gumihos are legendary nine-tailed fox creatures who, when disguised in humanoid form, use charm and guile to lure prey deep into the forest before revealing their true form and striking. The green-eyed canids use brutally sharp fangs to tear out their victim's throat and feast on their liver. Those few who survive a gumiho's trap recall the canid's eerie green eyes as well as a strange marble amulet warn around the monster's neck. Old stories claim that a fox transforms into a gumiho once it has lived for a thousand years, quadrupling in size, growing eight additional tails, and gaining the power to change shape. Terrifyingly, it also gains an insatiable hunger for the livers of people. This hunger is so closely connected to a gumiho's power that if it abstains from eating liver for one thousand days, the gumiho loses its magic and permanently takes on its humanoid guise—also losing its evil nature in the process. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Gumiho](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1413) **Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 81 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +33, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +30 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 **Guileful Charm** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Any creature that converses with a gumiho must attempt a DC 35 Will save. The creature then becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected but knows the gumiho tried to charm it.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is affected by the failure effects of 4th-level [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34).
**Critical Failure** The target is affected by the failure effects of [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87). **Items** _fox marble_ --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +32 **Will** +29 **HP** 310 **Immunities** [charm](/Traits.aspx?ID=247) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15 --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** claw +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d8+18 slashing **Melee** jaws +31 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d12+18 piercing **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61) - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104) (3 slots) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) (4 slots) - **5th** [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=314) (3 slots) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=254) (3 slots) - **7th** [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) (2 slots) - **8th** [Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=73), [Power Word Stun](/Spells.aspx?ID=228) (2 slots) - **9th** [Power Word Kill](/Spells.aspx?ID=227) (1 slot) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The gumiho changes into [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) or fox shape. Their humanoid shape has a specific, persistent appearance but their true form is their fox shape. In humanoid shape, the gumiho uses their original size, loses their jaws strike but retains their claws strike, and their Speed is reduced to 25 feet. A gumiho is never able to completely conceal one of their nine tails through this transformation. **Sapping Critical** **Trigger** The gumiho critically hits with a claw or jaws Strike; **Effect** The gumiho uses its _fox marble_ to channel the life force lost by the target and heal for half the amount of damage dealt.
Fox Marble A gumiho wears a strange green amulet around its neck. This fox marble stores all of the gumiho's knowledge. By focusing on the marble, a gumiho can perfectly recall anything it has experienced in its lifetime. A creature who manages to steal a gumiho's fox marble is lucky indeed, for they can also use it to access the gumiho's memories. However, if the gumiho still lives, the vengeful fox will stop at nothing to recover its marble. Whoever wears a fox marble gains a +2 item bonus to all checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26).
name: Gumiho
