Mountain guides whisper stories of giant white birds that stretch towards the sky, singing with human voices. While these stories are often dismissed as tall tales, the truth is much stranger, for the bird they've encountered not only has a human voice, but one a human face as well. Inmyeonjos are enormous white birds with flowing tails, abnormally long necks, and mask-like human faces. They wear an elongated black headpiece that serves as a vessel for their life energy, enabling their reincarnation through any living creature. Inmyeonjos often serve as gatekeepers or guardians of the tombs of great sages and leaders who brought peace to their lands and people. They can also be found atop on high mountains where the border between earth and sky is thinnest. Ultimately, inmyeonjos strive for balance and peace among all mortals. A person who has encountered an inmyeonjo often has trouble remembering if the strange creature was beautiful or terrifying. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Inmyeonjo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1414) **Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 82 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +32, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +32, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +32, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +27 **Str** +6 **Dex** +9 **Con** +7 **Int** +7 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +5 **Blurred Countenance** Creatures farther than 60 feet from an inmyeonjo see it only as the generic form of a huge bird. An inmyeonjo has [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) against attacks originating farther than 60 feet away. --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +28 **Will** +30 **HP** 300 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) **Limited Immortality** Though an inmyeonjo still needs to eat, drink, and sleep, they don't age and can't die of old age.
**Terrifying Beauty** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet. Any creature within 60 feet of an inmyeonjo that the inmyeonjo can see must attempt a DC 32 Will save. The effects last for 1 day while it is within the area of blurred countenance.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is immune to the inmyeonjo's Terrifying Beauty for 1 day.
**Success** The creature finds the inmyeonjo beguiling and becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37).
**Failure** The creature finds the inmyeonjo terrifying and becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Critical Failure** The creature finds the inmyeonjo dreadful, becoming frightened 2. They can't willingly move closer to the inmyeonjo. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 100 feet **Melee** talon + 30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+18 bludgeoning **Melee** tail +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+18 bludgeoning **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 34, attack +26 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190) - **2nd** [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) (3 slots) - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Zone of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=379) (3 slots) - **4th** [Discern Lies](/Spells.aspx?ID=74), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247) (3 slots) - **5th** [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=327) (2 slots) - **6th** [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=218), [Vibrant Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=357) (2 slots) - **7th** [Ethereal Jaunt](/Spells.aspx?ID=105), [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=185) (2 slots) - **8th** [Discern Location](/Spells.aspx?ID=75), [Unrelenting Observation](/Spells.aspx?ID=351) (1 slot)
Material Reincarnation When an inmyeonjo dies, their body rapidly decomposes to dust, but their black headpiece remains. The headpiece magically fits itself to any size head. A creature that dons the headpiece becomes immune to [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and stops aging, but cannot remove it by nonmagical means (DC 37 check to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371)). Over the course of 100 years, the creature grows feathers and their neck elongates until they eventually become an inmyeonjo.
name: Inmyeonjo

<title level="1" pfs="">[Lophiithu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1415)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Lophiithus dwell in the deepest ocean trenches, places untouched by light, hope, or mortal comprehension. They appear to be gaping maws with distended jaws, vestigial flaps of putrefying flesh, and filamentous tentacles tipped with glowing light. Their extraplanar mouths take up most of their bodies, leaving no room for other organs, and any prey they swallow is transported to the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19) for digestion.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 44

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Lophiithu.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 21">[Lophiithu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1415)</title>

[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +40, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +43, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +40, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +36

---

**AC** 43

**Ref** +36 

**Will** +38 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 400

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, swim 80 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly 3d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
lure +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d6+18 bludgeoning plus 2d6 mental and beguiling lure

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 41
- **Cantrips (9th)**
[Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61)
- **8th**
[Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158) (×2), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) (×2)
- **9th**
[Telepathic Demand](/Spells.aspx?ID=336) (×2), [Unfathomable Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=349) (×2)
- **10th**
[Fabricated Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=106), [Gate](/Spells.aspx?ID=130) (at will)
- **Constant (9th)**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Beguiling Lure**  ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) A creature hit by a lophiithu's lure must succeed at a DC 41 Will save or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with the lophiithu's lures for 1 minute. On a critical failure, it can't use reactions while it remains fascinated. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 hour.

**Dimensional Swallow**   The lophiithu's digestive organs exist in the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19), regardless of the location of their external form. The lophiithu can swallow any number of creatures of any size. Creatures that [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) the lophiithu's stomach do so normally. Creatures that cut themselves free instead find themselves in the Maelstrom near nothing but one of the lophiithu's disembodied stomachs. When the lophiithu dies, their stomach becomes a rift connecting to their mouth, allowing a creature to return to their plane. This rift disappears after 1 minute.

**Lure Whip** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The lophiithu makes a lure Strike against each creature within reach. These attacks count toward the lophiithu's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after these attacks.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** <actions string="Single Action" />  Gargantuan, 3d12+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 44 and dimensional swallow

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Planar Digestion</title>

A lophiithu's stomachs are scattered across islands in the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19). Extraplanar scholars remain unclear as to how these stomachs grow independently of a lophiithu, but are unsurprised by the planar abilities of the stomachs. Expeditions into the Maelstrom have turned up stomachs ranging from as small as a human fist to as large as mountains, suggesting the existence of enormous lophiithus.
</aside>
name: Lophiithu

An orochi is an enormous serpent with eight heads and eight tails that lives among large waterways. Orochi are vile, amoral creatures kept at bay only with yearly sacrifices. Most settlements unable to satisfy an orochi with a sacrifice soon meet their destruction. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Orochi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1416) **Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 84 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +31, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +29 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +10 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 42 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) **Fort** +33 **Ref** +30 **Will** +27 **HP** 400 ((body) ; **Resistances** poison 15
HP (head) 60, multiple heads ) **Immunities** area damage **Resistances** poison 15 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet, DC 37
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Multiple Heads** A creature can attempt to sever one of the orochi's eight heads by specifically targeting it and dealing damage equal to the head's HP. A head not completely severed returns to full HP at the end of the attacking creature's turn. An orochi that loses all of their heads immediately dies.
**Multiple Opportunities** The orochi gains an extra reaction per round for each of their heads that remains beyond the first, which it can use only to make Attacks of Opportunity. They can't use more than 1 reaction on the same triggering action, and the orochi must use a different head for each Attack of Opportunity. --- **Speed** 30 feet, swim 60 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([reach 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+18 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tail +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+18 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Ranged** rock +34 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+18 bludgeoning **Orochi Flurry** The orochi makes a number of jaws Strikes equal to the number of their remaining heads, each against a different target. The orochi's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the orochi makes all of their attacks. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge, 2d12+18 bludgeoning, Rupture 40
Spoils of the Orochi Legends state that a warrior able to slay an orochi in single combat will find a blade deep within the creature's flesh. Such blades are said to have a mastery over flames and to be able to cut entire fields of grass in a single stroke.
name: Orochi

markdown:
Those who feast on the sun are said to carry its power within them. Sanzuwus are small crows with red plumage and dark throats. According to legend, they stole their burning power from the sun and now fly across the sky as blinding streaks of fire. When they land on the earth, it ignites around them, causing unbound destruction. A handful of people worship these radiant birds and inscribe their three-legged visage on coins, items, and weapons. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Sanzuwu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1417) **Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 85 **Perception** +26; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +25 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +29 **Will** +23 **HP** 295 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 15 **Golden Sun** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 10 feet. A golden aura envelopes the sanzuwu, protecting them from weak magic. Any non-[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) spells of 3rd level or lower burn away and are ineffective. A sanzuwu can deactivate or activate the golden aura as a free action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**Scorching Gaze** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet targets the sanzuwu or one of their allies with a weapon attack; **Effect** The sanzuwu focuses their gaze on the triggering creature's weapon, heating it to a burning temperature for an instant. The creature takes 4d8 fire damage unless the creature [Releases](/Actions.aspx?ID=83) the weapon as a free action. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** beak +30 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6+14 piercing plus 3d6 fire **Melee** talon +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d4+14 piercing plus 3d6 fire **Fiery Transformation** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The sanzuwu transforms into an enormous bird made of flame. As the sanzuwu begins their transformation, creatures within a 10-foot emanation take 6d8 fire damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). While in their flame form, the sanzuwu gains the effects of 4th-level [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102). They can use Fiery Transformation again to revert back to their standard form. **Radiant Blast** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The sanzuwu opens their mouth to produce a bead of burning energy, hurling it a point within 200 feet. The bead detonates, dealing 7d8 fire damage and 4d10 sonic damage to all creatures in a 30-foot burst (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that critically fail become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round. The sanzuwu can't use Radiant Blast again for 1d4 rounds. **Scorch Earth** **Requirements** The sanzuwu is standing on the ground; **Effect** The sanzuwu Strides. A 6th-level [_wall of fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) appears in all the spaces the sanzuwu left during this movement, lasting for 1 round. The sanzuwu can't use Scorch Earth again for 1d4 rounds. **Transfixing Sunbeam** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Requirements** The sanzuwu is in their fiery form; **Effect** The sanzuwu spreads their wings, emitting a bright cascade of light in a 30-foot cone. Creatures in the cone must attempt a DC 33 Fortitude save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 2.
A Fiery Murder When several sanzuwus are together the immense heat radiating from them can cause an inferno of devastation. Legends tell of sanzuwus gathered together as the ground rends into volcanic rifts and uncontrollable blazes. Though they bring destruction in their wake, some stories claim that a brave individual who can follow these cataclysmic rifts for 10 days will reach the sun and earn its blessing
name: Sanzuwu

<title level="1" pfs="">[Spirit Turtle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1418)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Spirit turtles are benevolent fey known to heal those in need and confer other powerful gifts. These gentle giants resemble enormous turtles and often bear entire ecosystems on their shells.

 In ancient times, so the legend goes, spirit turtles used to grant wishes to many mortals. After millennia of such encounters, the giant creatures grew tired and went into hiding or returned to their home plane, the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10). Those few spirit turtles who remain on Golarion now grant their favors only to the purest and worthiest mortal petitioners.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 47

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/SpiritTurtle.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 21">[Spirit Turtle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1418)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="CG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 86

**Perception** +35

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +36, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +34, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +40, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +36, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +40

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +6

**Con** +11

**Int** +6

**Wis** +10

**Cha** +7
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 45 

**Fort** +40 

**Ref** +32 

**Will** +38 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 320

, regeneration 25 (deactivated by [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) or evil)

</row>

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 20, physical 15

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 20

**Pacifying Aura** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 100 feet. A spirit turtle exudes a constant feeling of calm. Creatures within the aura are subjected to a 10th-level [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=31) spell (DC 44 Will save). The effects persist up to 1 hour, even if the creature leaves the aura. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 hour.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, swim 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d12+14 piercing plus 2d6 energy and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foot +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d10+14 bludgeoning plus 2d6 energy and [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28)

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 44
- **4th**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (at will), [Vital Beacon](/Spells.aspx?ID=359) (at will)
- **5th**
[Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=29) (×3)
- **6th**
[Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) (×2)
- **7th**
[Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self only), [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=248) (×2)
- **8th**
[Moment of Renewal](/Spells.aspx?ID=201) (×2)
- **9th**
[Field of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=114) (×2)
- **10th**
[Primal Phenomenon](/Spells.aspx?ID=231), [Revival](/Spells.aspx?ID=262)
- **Constant (10th)**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  4d12+7 bludgeoning, DC 41

**Environmental Balance** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** The spirit turtle begins their turn; **Effect** The spirit turtle harnesses their connection with the world around them to augment their attacks. They choose acid, cold, electricity, or fire. The energy damage dealt by their Strikes and Unbalancing Stomp changes to the selected energy type.

**Unbalancing Stomp** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The spirit turtle makes a foot Strike. Whether or not the Strike hits, the stomp creates a shockwave on the ground in a 15-foot burst centered on the target. All creatures in the area other than the spirit turtle take 10d10 bludgeoning and 4d8 energy damage and are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that critically succeed at their Reflex save are not knocked prone. The spirit turtle can't use Unbalancing Stomp again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Spiritual Tasks</title>

Most spirit turtles grant an extended lifetime as gift for completing an important task. The requirements of a task vary from turtle to turtle, but they are always massive undertakings. In one part of the saga of Artimos the Tailor, Artimos met with a spirit turtle and earned their favor by sewing a quilt as massive as the lake in which the turtle slept. The quilt required cloth from the robes of each ruler in Tian Xia, but Artimos's efforts eventually earned him a golden needle to aid him on his journeys.
</aside>
name: Spirit Turtle

The Wall of Heaven claims many intrepid climbers. Some such travelers come seeking fortune. Others seek enlightenment. Many, however, find only an icy death. With no means for a proper burial, many of these unfortunate souls remain abandoned to the ice, their bodies becoming macabre guideposts along mountain trails. Through means unknown, the most willful of these frozen travelers reanimate to seek vengeance upon the living for their denied burial. Such creatures, called sthiras, are composed of multiple bodies or body parts frozen together into one horrific monstrosity. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Sthira](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1419) **Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 87 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +39 **Str** +9 **Dex** +2 **Con** +10 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -2 **Items** _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)_ --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +39 **Ref** +27 **Will** +33 **HP** 475 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 20 **Freezing Death** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 20 feet. A creature that starts its turn in the aura takes 6d6 cold damage (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude). If the creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) by the sthira, it takes one degree of success worse than it rolled.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Frozen to the Touch** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) A creature that damages the sthira with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack, tries to [Grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=184) it, or otherwise touches it takes 6d6 cold damage (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _greatsword_ +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+15 slashing plus 2d6 cold and frostbite **Melee** fist +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+15 bludgeoning plus 2d6 cold, frostbite, and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 39, attack +31 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245) - **9th** [Polar Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=224) (×2), [Storm of Vengeance](/Spells.aspx?ID=313) (hail only each round), [Wall of Ice](/Spells.aspx?ID=364) - **10th** [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=186) **Frostbite** A creature that takes cold damage from a sthira's Strikes or aura must attempt a DC 42 Fortitude save. A creature that already has [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from frostbite ignores this effect.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, and is temporarily immune to this ability for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature takes 3d6 persistent cold damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). **Create Sthira** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a creature dies while it has [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the sthira's frostbite, the creature rises as a sthira in 24 hours. If the creature is [grappled](/Traits.aspx?ID=184) by the sthira when it dies, the dying creature becomes permanently fused with the sthira instead. In this case, the sthira immediately recovers a number of Hit Points equal to the half the dead creature's maximum Hit Points.
Other Sthiras Mountaineer stories warn of sthiras that have incorporated the bodies of powerful non-humanoids and have even more terrifying abilities as a result, ranging from the capacity to cast spells, exhale gouts of ice, or even fly.
name: Sthira

Algeas protect (and sometimes retrieve) souls at risk of being claimed by other entities, particularly spellcasters who dabbled with otherworldly forces but never officially pledged their souls. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Algea](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1420) **Source** [Malevolence](/Sources.aspx?ID=88) pg. 70 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 60 feet, see magic **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +21, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22 **Str** -1 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **See Magic** An algea can see magic auras on creatures or objects that it can observe; it can't see magic auras on creatures or objects that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) from its view. It learns the schools of magic for all spells affecting creatures. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +24 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 144 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36) **Resistances** bludgeoning 10, [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) 10, piercing 10, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) 10, slashing 5 **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 5 **Grieving Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. A creature that begins its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 27 Will save. [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) take a –2 circumstance penalty to this saving throw. The creature is then temporarily immune to the grieving aura for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** For 1 round, the creature can't use reactions and is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for that turn as it sobs uncontrollably.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effects lasts while the creature remains in the aura plus 1 minute. --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 40 feet **Divine Innate Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=329) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self and soul caged creature only; to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) only) **Rituals** DC 30 - **5th** [Call Spirit](/Rituals.aspx?ID=5) **Drain Magic** ([Abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) Whenever a creature fails its save against Frenzied Beaks, the algea attempts to counter a spell effect active on that creature (6th level, [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check +22). The algea can choose which effect to attempt to counteract for each creature. **Frenzied Beaks** Each foe in the swarm's space takes 5d6 piercing damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex) and is exposed to drain magic. This damage affects [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures as if it had the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune. **Soul Cage** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) An [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) damaged by Frenzied Beaks must attempt a DC 30 Will save.
**Critical Success** The undead is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The undead takes a –10-foot status penalty to Speeds for 1 round.
**Failure** As success, but the undead is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 round, although it can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 30).
**Critical Failure** The undead is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) for 1 round. The algea can attempt to [_plane shift_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) with the restrained creature even if that creature isn't willing, but an unwilling creature can attempt a Will save to resist _plane shift_. If it succeeds, the algea plane shifts and leaves the incorporeal creature behind, which is then no longer restrained.
One of Many The individual psychopomps that comprise an algea swarm are particularly minuscule nosoi psychopomps. If statistics for a single such creature become necessary, treat it as a [nosoi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=339) psychopomp with the [weak adjustment](/Rules.aspx?ID=791).
name: Algea

<title level="1" pfs="">[Tanglebones](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1421)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sometimes, when murderer buries a large number of victims in a mass grave, the vengeful spirits of the slain can become similarly tangled together. When these spirits can't untangle, they instead return to their physical remains, fusing into one horrific mass of bones and black, tar-like sludge—a tanglebones.

 One awful tale speaks of a bitter old dowager who learned that, through a legal loophole, ownership of her estate would revert to her estranged daughter. She murdered her 13 servants, buried their bodies in the house's basement, and then played the gracious mother in handing over the manor keys at the appointed time. One night, the tanglebones that arose from the dowager's atrocity murdered the daughter and her family. By the time the authorities learned of what happened, the dowager had fled the nation, changed her identity, and (the story goes) wasted no time rebuilding her fortunes—this time becoming a queen.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Tanglebones.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Tanglebones](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1421)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Malevolence](/Sources.aspx?ID=88) pg. 71

**Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** -2

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +17 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 145
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyze](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
bludgeoning 7

**Ectoplasmic Filament**<actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature within the tanglebone's reach uses an action with the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action, or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** A filament of black, oily ectoplasm lashes out at the triggering creature, dealing 2d8+8 slashing damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). If the filament deals damage, the tanglebones gains 2d8 temporary Hit Points.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hurled bone +17 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning

**Eerie Flexibility**   A tanglebones can shift and adjust its mass to fit into smaller areas, allowing it to fit through tight spaces as if it were a Medium creature. While [Squeezing](/Actions.aspx?ID=31), it can move at its full Speed.

**Gnaw** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The tanglebones has [Grabbed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) a foe; **Effect** The tanglebones gnaws and chews on the [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) target with its many skulls, dealing 2d6+8 piercing damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). On a critical failure, the creature is also [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) from the pain.

**Skeletal Storm** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The tanglebones makes a single claw Strike against every creature in reach. These attacks count toward the tanglebones' multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the tanglebones makes all its attacks.

**Sprawled Bones** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) Until the next time it acts, the tanglebones appears as a sprawling mass of bones. It has an automatic result of 35 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to pass as a grisly but seemingly harmless mess.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Unravelers</title>

It's believed that a tanglebones's formation can be prevented if even one body in the mass grave died from a different cause than its fellows. In regions where these undead horrors are more common, additional bodies known as unravellers are often dropped into mass graves in an attempt to prevent a tanglebones from rising. 
</aside>

markdown:
Aigamuxas are towering humanoids that stalk arid deserts in search of prey. Carnivorous and voracious, they hunt anything that moves but especially relish eating sentient creatures. Many Mwangi people incorrectly refer to aigamuxas as demons, while others more accurately describe them as having descended from giants. Aigamuxas resemble large humans with smooth hollows where their eyes should be, but their eyes are actually embedded in the soles of their feet. They sport long, sharp claws and teeth that they use to tear their prey apart once they catch them. A moving aigamuxa's odd gait resembles dancing more than a typical walk or run, but its speed is alarming, if difficult to correctly estimate at a distance. An aigamuxa's unique physiology makes catching prey difficult, and most aigamuxas are constantly hungry. When pursuing prey, an aigamuxa must occasionally stop to lift its feet in order to regain their bearings. Most stand on their hands while looking around, which allows them to immediately backflip back into a run. An aigamuxa's eyes are generally resistant to the sands of their native desert habitats, but irritants such as chilies or caltrops can seriously impair their hunting ability, and desert travelers often carry a bag of these in case they need to deal with an aigamuxa. Clever aigamuxas know that attacking wandering prey can be very dangerous, instead using their powerful hands to dig deep into sand dunes or dirt and wait to ambush passersby. Aigamuxas are particularly good at hiding in their home environments, and unfortunate travelers often don't notice the barely visible eyes of an aigamuxa until it's too late. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Aigamuxa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1425) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 292 **Perception** +19; [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Limited Vision** An aigamuxa's eyes are located on the bottom of their feet, making it difficult for them to see. An aigamuxa is typically blind. If they [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84), they can see normally until the end of their turn.
**Weak Feet** If an aigamuxa takes damage from Striding or Stepping into hazardous terrain or a square with similar grounded hazards (such as [caltrops](/Equipment.aspx?ID=9)), they can't [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) until the end of their next turn. --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +13 **HP** 140 --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** jaws +20, **Damage** 2d12+9 piercing **Burrowed Ambush** **Requirements** The aigamuxa is [Hiding](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) in dirt, sand, or another soft surface; **Effect** The aigamuxa makes a claw Strike against a creature within reach. On a hit, the aigamuxa automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the creature. Whether or not they hit, the aigamuxa then Strides. If they have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), the creature moves with the aigamuxa. **Burrowing Concealment** **Requirement** The aigamuxa is standing on dirt, sand, or another soft surface; **Effect** The aigamuxa quickly digs into the surface and [Hides](/Actions.aspx?ID=62). They leave their feet partially exposed, allowing them to see out from the surface. The aigamuxa can hold their breath for up to 10 minutes while hiding in this way. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Small, 2d12+4 bludgeoning, Rupture 22
The First Aigamuxas Stories say aigamuxas were once giants who entered into a wager with [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=11), claiming they could stand on their hands longer than any other creature. Lamashtu produced a simple chimpanzee, pointed to its feet and called them hands. The giants could not hold their positions and fell. Lamashtu moved their eyes to their feet and told them, “If you wish to boast of your ability, let this be your blessing.”
name: Aigamuxa

markdown:
Asanbosams are monstrous, hairy humanoids with [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) fangs and muscular limbs that end in powerful, hooked claws. They hang in treetops from their hooked feet, waiting patiently for a creature to pass by below. Asanbosams grab their prey from above, latching onto their unfortunate victims' necks to drain their blood. They react to perceived intrusions with belligerence, stalking visitors from high branches. Locals tell horror stories of these “bloodsucking tree-folk,” and experienced explorers keep a close watch on the treetops in asanbosam territory. Visitors to the Expanse often compare asanbosams to vampires, but these horrors aren't undead and bear no relation. An asanbosam's diet calls for a large amount of iron (typically in the form of blood) to maintain the integrity of its oversized cold iron teeth. When not hunting or mating, an asanbosam spends its waking hours chewing on certain types of rocks to sharpen its teeth. Some folk tales claim that asanbosams are as old as time and the original source of cold iron, and that veins of the metal found in the ground are truly the graveyards of ancient asanbosams. Brave (or foolish) poachers often infiltrate asanbosam territory to hunt these monsters in the hope of collecting sacks of their valuable teeth. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Asanbosam](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1426) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 293, [Pathfinder #146: Cult of Cinders](/Sources.aspx?ID=7) pg. 80 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** -3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +10 **HP** 95 --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** cold iron jaws +17 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174)), **Damage** 2d8+7 piercing **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+5 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Attack from Above** **Requirements** The asanbosam is positioned above a creature; **Effect** The asanbosam makes a claw Strike with its [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) increased to 15 feet. On a hit, the Strike deals an additional 1d6 slashing damage and the asanbosam immediately [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target and pulls it to a space adjacent to the asanbosam. This counts as two attacks for the asanbosam's multiple attack penalty. **Drink Blood** **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within reach of the asanbosam's jaws; **Effect** The asanbosam sinks its teeth into the creature to drink its blood. If the victim is grabbed, this is automatic; otherwise, the asanbosam must succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC. The victim becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and the asanbosam regains 10 HP, gaining any excess Hit Points beyond its maximum as temporary Hit Points. These temporary Hit Points remain for 1 minute. Drinking Blood from a creature that's already drained increases the victim's drained value by 1 to a maximum of drained 3, but doesn't restore any HP to the asanbosam.
A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 each week. A blood transfusion, which requires a successful DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check and a blood donor or sufficient blood, reduces the drained value by 1 after 10 minutes.
Asanbosam Treasure The corpse of a dead asanbosam is a treasure in itself. Extracting the creature's [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) fangs is no easy process and requires either powerful extraction tools or a vat of chemicals to melt the surrounding bone tissue. The payoff, however, can be substantial in the right market.
name: Asanbosam

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Eloko](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1429)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

If a biloko is lucky enough to consume several spellcasters or other creatures with innate magical abilities, they undergo a subtle transformation over the course of 1 week. Such a fey is known as an eloko, and in addition to their incredible appetite (even by biloko standards), they have the supernatural ability to grow to immense size.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Even with the Gorilla King's fall, many charau-ka still worship the terrible might of the god [Angazhan](/Deities.aspx?ID=72). The strongest of these acolytes guard the steps of the High Throne in Usaro, ensuring that no one without Angazhan's favor or suitable physical prowess can approach the top of the massive stone step pyramid.

While charau-ka are infamous for their brutality, some even frighten their own kind in their obsession with violence. These deadly butchers are well-versed in the art of inflicting vicious, bloody wounds on their enemies, and they consider anyone not charau-ka an enemy. A single taste of a foe's blood can grant one terrifying strength, as if the charau-ka butcher literally gains power from their victims' pain. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Charau-ka Butcher](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1432) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 297, [Pathfinder #146: Cult of Cinders](/Sources.aspx?ID=7) pg. 85 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45)_ --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +12 **Will** +13 **HP** 95 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Blood Fury** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Trigger** The charau-ka butcher deals bleed damage to a creature; **Effect** The charau-ka licks blood from their weapon, becoming furious. They regain 5 Hit Points and gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls until the end of their turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** _trident_ +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing **Melee** fist +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** _trident_ +16 ([deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing **Ranged** thrown debris +15 ([deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning **Mauler** While the charau-ka butcher is raging, their melee Strikes deal an additional 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). **Rage** As the [barbarian](/Classes.aspx?ID=2) class ability; AC 22, +9 Hit Points, +2 melee damage **Shrieking Frenzy** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** The charau-ka's turn begins; **Effect** The charau-ka is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) until the end of their turn and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike. While in the frenzy, the charau-ka can't speak and automatically critically fails [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks, due to their loud wailing. **Thrown Weapon Mastery** Any weapon a charau-ka throws gains the [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) weapon trait. Furthermore, when a charau-ka throws an improvised weapon, they do not take the –2 penalty for doing so, nor does they take a penalty for using a thrown improvised weapon with the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait to make a lethal attack.
name: Charau-ka Butcher

markdown:
While it's easy to mistake a grootslang for an elephant upon first glance, once the rest of its body becomes visible, the creature resembles a grotesque puppet manipulated like a marionette from the inside by a snake large enough to swallow a building. A grootslang stands 20 feet tall, with skin like plated armor and a pair of massive forearms. It sports six deadly tusks capable of piercing flesh and bone with ease. The rest of its body extends up to 60 feet long. A grootslang's face resembles that of an elephant, but much like its serpentine cousins, it can unhinge its lower jaw to swallow its prey. A person encountering a grootslang has a slight chance of reasoning with or bribing the beast, possibly escaping a deadly fate. A hoard of gems is no guarantee of safety, however, and a particularly cruel grootslang will just as likely go back on their word upon receiving the offering. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Grootslang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1433) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 298 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +8 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +0 **Deep Breath** A grootslang can hold its breath for 1 hour. --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +27 **Will** +26 **HP** 370 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 40 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** foot +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+15 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+15 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tail +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+15 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tusk +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+15 piercing **Aquatic Elusion** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Requirements** The grootslang is fully immersed in water; **Effect** The grootslang teleports to another body of water within 1 mile that is wide enough to hold the grootslang. **Defensive Coil** The grootslang coils its body. It gains 20 resistance against physical attacks until the start of its next turn. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** tail only, 2d12+12 bludgeoning, DC 34 **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** jaws only, Huge, 3d12+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 37 **Thunderous Slam** The grootslang makes a foot Strike against a creature in reach. Whether or not the Strike hits, all creatures within 10 feet of the grootslang must attempt a DC 37 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of its turn.
**Failure** The creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and takes 2d8 bludgeoning damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature takes 4d8 bludgeoning damage.
Unwise Creations The grootslang sounds like a cruel cosmic prank by the gods of creation. Tales among the Mwangi peoples claim that when Golarion was young, an unwise and now forgotten deity created beasts with unmatched strength and cunning, which became the first grootslangs. These creatures proved too fearsome, and the deities separated them to create the first elephants and snakes. One of these grootslangs escaped, however, and the deities were unable to stop grootslangs from repopulating as they hid among the Mwangi Expanse.
name: Grootslang

markdown:
K'nonna are half human—and only half, in the most literal sense. They most often resemble the right half of a human, and less frequently the left half, split perfectly down the middle starting at the crown of their head. They accordingly have one eye, one ear, one arm, one leg, half of a nose, and half of a mouth full of razor-sharp teeth. A faint shimmer in the air, like the waves over a hot stone, hints at where their other half would be. Despite the bizarre appearance of the k'nonna, they have no trouble balancing or performing everyday tasks that might be impeded by missing half of their bodies. They are extremely dexterous, able to perform complex tasks with only a single hand. The nature of their missing half has so far defied explanation; it is not invisible, nor does it exist on another plane, yet it seems to exert enough force for a k'nonna to walk like a human being. No one is certain of the k'nonna's origins, except that they are not of this world and not descended from any known mortal creature. Banishment magic has no effect on them, implying they do not come from another plane. They are intelligent but difficult to communicate with—they speak only in half words and hear only half of what anyone says. K'nonna are also much less interested in talking than eating, and humanoids happen to be their favorite meal. The remains of a k'nonna's victim are unmistakable, as they only consume half the body. Stories tell of the half-people's love for gifts, especially jewelry. Offerings of rings and necklaces easily distract a k'nonna long enough for prey to escape. Particularly bold victims might try to trick a k'nonna into wearing a bracelet that binds their one visible wrist or ankle to immobilize them, though the tactic is not foolproof, as a k'nonna displeased with their gifts will hunt the person who displeased them for the rest of their days. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [K'nonna](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1434) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 299 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39); half, see Half Speaker **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +11 **Will** +11 **HP** 140 **Gilded Fascination** When offered a gift worth at least 50 gp, the k'nonna must attempt a DC 20 Will saving throw; if the gift is worth at least 100 gp, the DC is instead 25. If they fail the save, the k'nonna accepts the gift, becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) until the end of their next turn, and cannot attack while fascinated. On a critical failure, the fascination lasts for 1 minute. Offering a gift is an Interact action, and offering multiple gifts in the same round has no additional effect.
**Half-Speaker** A k'nonna can communicate easily with other k'nonna, but when communicating with other creatures, only half of the spoken words make sense. In order to convey information to a k'nonna or understand what they're saying, a non-k'nonna must succeed at a DC 20 Perception check.
**Half Steps** Even though k'nonna are split in half, they still move and Stride as though they have two arms and legs. However, binding just one wrist or ankle leaves them incapable of using that limb, as if it were bound to another. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +20, **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Pounce** The k'nonna Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the k'nonna began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), they remain hidden until after this ability's Strike.
Strange Origins In ancient times, k'nonna were simply said to come from “another world.” However, even as Mwangi scholars gain a greater understanding of the galaxy and multiverse at large, they have been unable to determine what world that could be. No discovered planets have any known species like them, and k'nonna don't come from any known plane. The very existence of k'nonna stands as proof that the universe is still much wider than anyone knows.
name: K'nonna

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pygmy Kaava](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1435)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An unusually large number of kaava living in the Kaava Lands are relatively small, standing about 3 feet tall and weighing about 30 pounds. Pygmy kaava openly show their disdain for larger kaava, and the two groups rarely get along. Pygmy kaava often hunt alongside trained bush vipers or black mamba snakes.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Kaava hunting parties can number in the dozens, although they blend easily into the forest and cover all traces of their passage. While community elders assign various tasks to hunting parties, such as recovering food or deterring intruders, the parties are expected to accomplish these tasks as they see fit.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Karina](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1437)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Karinas—also known as plague birds—are large owl-like creatures with dark red feathers, powerful talons, and unsettling eyes. Their broad wings conceal a pair of wiry humanoid arms that end in clawed fingers, which they use to dig through burial sites in search of corpses. The fell purpose of this is still a mystery, though most believe that karinas make off with these dead bodies to consume them in the safety of their lairs. The difficulty of tracking the beasts, as well as the danger, makes it difficult to know for sure.

 A karina's terrifying appearance suggests that they're not natural creatures. Many legends claim that karinas first flew out of [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12) to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) to consume the corpses of the first mortals, while others state that the first karina was born from the soul of the first mortal who partook in cannibalism. The power of good repulses karinas, suggesting an evil or fiendish origin.

 Karinas are plague carriers and intentionally attack creatures to spread their own personal pestilence. The supernatural disease they carry, known as grave blight, causes fever, disorientation, and (at later stages) an innate desire to attack others and spread the disease. Karinas revel in the slow collapse of villages, lurking in the surrounding jungle to observe every miserable moment. As the locals bury their dead, the karinas swoop in to steal the corpses.

 In addition to carrying a terrible disease, karinas also have the ability to mimic voices. They typically repeat phrases they've heard, but twist the voice into a fiendish corruption of the original. Intelligent enough to recognize the friends and family of a possible target, a karina will use the mocking voice of a loved one to frighten them. When terrorizing a village, they generally work together in groups of two or three.

</column>

**Mocking Cry** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16)) The karina mimics a voice and mocks a creature within 30 feet. They attempt a Deception check to Demoralize the creature. Regardless of whether the check succeeds, the target creature is temporarily immune to the karina's Mocking Cry for 1 hour.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Grave Karinas</title>

Legends speak of powerful karinas that have stolen the corpses of important figures or powerful creatures and consumed part of the corpse's soul in the process. The soul empowers the karina, which becomes a grave karina. Legends describe grave karinas as more savage in appearance and prone to attack all creatures, including other karinas. They supposedly have the power to manipulate others through their shadows and can breathe burning ash to burn their victims.
</aside>

A maliadi resembles a massive hippopotamus surrounded by a collar of flame. Maliadis are arrogant and tyrannical, and demand tribute from everyone they encounter—whether lone travelers or entire villages—to avert destruction. Many pay them off, for maliadis can hurl parts of their flaming collars to form geysers of flame or fiery walls, and they're also powerful shapeshifters. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Maliadi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1438) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 302 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +29, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +29 **Str** +9 **Dex** +3 **Con** +9 **Int** +1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 **Deep Breath** The maliadi can hold its breath for 1 day. --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +25 **Will** +28 **HP** 285 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) **Collar of Fire** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The maliadi has resistance 20 to physical ranged attacks, such as arrows, bolts, javelins, and thrown rocks, as such attacks are magically diverted through its collar of fire. Nonmagical weapons and ammunition affected by this ability are incinerated and destroyed.
**Counterspelling Wall** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Trigger** A creature within 100 feet of the maliadi that the maliadi can see Casts a Spell that targets the maliadi; **Effect** The maliadi casts one of its innate [_wall of fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) spells, with a duration of 1 round. If the spell that triggered this reaction passes through the wall of fire, the wall attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) it, with a counteract modifier of +28. --- **Speed** 30 feet, swim 20 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +35 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+19 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** foot +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) - **4th** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [Gaseous Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=129) - **8th** [Fire Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=118), [Volcanic Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=360) - **9th** [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) (×3), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=365) (×3) - **Constant (7th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **Constant (8th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Capsize** The maliadi tries to capsize an adjacent aquatic vessel of its size or smaller. The maliadi must succeed at a DC 35 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check (reduced by 5 for each size smaller the vessel is than the maliadi) or the pilot's [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) DC, whichever is higher. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** The maliadi can take on the appearance of any Large, Huge, or Gargantuan [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9). This doesn't change its Speed or Strikes. **River Form** The maliadi takes on the form of a stretch of river, with the effects of [_wind walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=376), except the maliadi can only affect itself, appears as a section of a flowing river that takes up the same space as the maliadi, and can't leave the ground. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** Huge, 3d10+17 bludgeoning, Rupture 35 **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 38
Monstrous Pride Due to their boundless arrogance, maliadis are unlikely to associate with others of their kind for extended periods, although they readily accept the services of smaller creatures they think might prove useful or expendable. They also dislike remaining in any form that doesn't convey their size and majesty for long. Those who know of a maliadi's hubris can often exploit it, such as by demanding a trial by combat to force the maliadi to leave an area alone.
name: Maliadi

markdown:
Mamlambos are river predators with hypnotic, bioluminescent skin. They appear similar to crocodiles, but the narrow head at the end of their long necks resembles a horse's skull, and their four legs are stubby and inefficient for land movement. Their skin's green glow is only barely visible in daylight or when underwater, though the glow is difficult to resist. As a result, they're frequently trailed by fish, birds, or other river creatures ensnared by the alluring radiance. Humanoids are mamlambos' favorite prey, particularly [humans](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), although they strangely prefer to only eat their victims' faces and brains. Mamlambos are dim-witted but sly, and sometimes hide their victims under submerged rocks or logs to make their discovery less likely. A mamlambo might be active in a region for days or even weeks before its presence is discovered. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Mamlambo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1439) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 303 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Luminous Lure** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 30 feet. The mamlambo emits dim light from its skin. A creature that enters or begins its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 23 Will save. [Humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) take a –2 penalty to this saving throw. Regardless of the save's result, the creature is temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), and for as long as it is in the aura it must spend at least 1 of its actions on each of its turns to move closer to the mamlambo as expediently as possible, while avoiding obvious dangers. If the creature ends its movement or turn adjacent to the mamlambo, it is slowed 1 until it is out of the aura or until the mamlambo makes a Strike against it. The target can attempt a new Will save at the beginning of each of their subsequent turns, and on a success, the effect ends.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature must spend each of its actions moving closer to the mamlambo, and if it ends its movement or turn adjacent to the mamlambo, it is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) until it is out of the aura or until the mamlambo makes a Strike against it. --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +20 **Will** +15 **HP** 155 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 10 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+11 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tail +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning **[Aquatic Ambush](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=2)** **Face Eater** **Requirements** The mamlambo has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), or there is a dying, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) creature within the mamlambo's reach; **Effect** The mamlambo deals 2d8+11 piercing damage to the creature (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). On a failure, the creature also takes an additional 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), or 4d6 persistent bleed damage on a critical failure.
An Unusual Phobia Mamlambos have an unusually strong aversion to river otters, despite being much larger and more dangerous. Since mamlambos rarely remain in an area inhabited by otters for long, river-dwelling people in the Mwangi Expanse often keep trained otters to aid in fishing and to ward off the potential presence of a mamlambo.
name: Mamlambo

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Rompo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1440)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Rompos are unusual creatures that feed on corpses. They have thin, almost-skeletal bodies with strong rear legs that resemble a bear's, front legs like a badger's, and a head like a hare's with humanoid ears. A rompo tends to be timid on its own; it avoids other creatures and typically flees in the presence of larger animals. Though no more intelligent than any other beast, it usually acts with extreme paranoia, dodging traps or ambushes with an uncanny acumen. When danger is present, a rompo utters a loud cry to afford itself space to run and hides by using its body's natural color-shifting fur as camouflage.

 Although rompos sport the perfect physical features to act as predators, they tend to live their lives as solitary scavengers of leftover corpses. Rompos prefer to avoid hunting for themselves since they risk sustaining injuries in the process. It's not uncommon for rompos to follow other predators for hours, waiting for an opportunity to feast on the leftover remains of a hunt.

 Once a rompo gains access to a meal, it takes several minutes circling the corpse to make sure the area is clear. As it begins to feast, it lets out a whimpering cry that fascinates and compels other creatures to keep away so that the rompo can feed in peace. Due to its constant feeding on decaying flesh, a rompo's mouth is filled with rot, parasites, and deadly pathogens. Its bite can impart these combined infections as a dangerous disease known as rompo chills. The disease causes its victim to tremble uncontrollably, lightly at first but eventually developing into a violent shaking that ends with the victim's death. Despite their cautious nature, rompos always seem to linger close to creatures they infect with rompo chills; though they remain unseen while the victim lives, they emerge when that victim finally succumbs, in order to feed on the corpse.

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +15,
**Damage** 2d8+5 piercing plus rompo chills

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claws +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 2d6+5 slashing

**Crooning Cry** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) 60 feet. The rompo hums in a low voice. Each creature within the emanation must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) and compelled to move away from the rompo, traveling to the furthest edge of the emanation. Fascinated creatures are also [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16). The effect lasts for 1 round, but if the rompo spends a single action on subsequent rounds, it can extend the duration by 1 round for all affected creatures. If the rompo attacks, the fascinated condition ends only for the creature that is attacked. On a successful save, a creature is temporarily immune to Crooning Cry for 24 hours.

**Rompo Chills**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) The target can't reduce its [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition while affected by rompo chills; **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 day; **Stage 1** [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (1 day); **Stage 2** clumsy 1 and sickened 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** clumsy 2 and sickened 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (1 day); **Stage 5** dead

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Rompo Packs</title>

Rompos tend to live in isolation, and on their own are so timid their behavior borders on cowardice. But in larger environments that can support them, like the Mwangi Jungle, rompos travel in packs of up to a dozen and become unusually aggressive. These packs actively hunt animals much larger than themselves, such as water buffalo, and one or two rompos cry through the night to deter other animals from their dens.
</aside>
name: Rompo
