Sié Goluo, sometimes referred to simply as Grandfather, is an ancient spirit that has kept watch over the peoples of the Mwangi Expanse for generations. Most believe he first appeared during the Age of Darkness to protect the Mwangi people from the worst dangers of the everlasting night. Bekyar storytellers claim he is older still, from a time before the first people walked Golarion. This immortal spirit has incarnated into multiple forms over millennia. In the oldest surviving tales, they say Sié Goluo looked like an ordinary—if oversized—ox, save for his nearly human-looking eyes. Most recently, people say he has the appearance of several different animals, but accounts vary on which animals make up his form. When a traveling farmer is assailed by a deadly beast, or a small village faces the wrath of an advancing army, Sié Goluo manifests in whatever form will allow him to best defend them. There seem to be no geographical limitations on where he can manifest, as there are tales of him saving Mwangi adventurers as far spread as Tian Xia and Arcadia. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 42 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Sié Goluo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1441) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 305 **Perception** +25; imprecise [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) 60 feet **Languages** [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +31, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +31, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +28 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +8 **Int** +1 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +20 **Will** +28 **HP** 320 (rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118); double resistane against non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When Sié Goluo is destroyed, he reforms, fully healed, at a location of his choosing anywhere on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) after 2d4 days. If every person of Mwangi descent rejects belief in Sié Goluo, the spirit is permanently destroyed.
**Ancestral Response** **Trigger** A creature on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) calls out for Sié Goluo; **Effect** Sié Goluo instantaneously appears in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of the triggering creature and heals 8d8 hit points on all allies that can see him within 60 feet. Allies healed this way gain a +2 circumstance bonus on saving throws for 1 minute. Creatures that receive these benefits become temporarily immune to them for 1 year.
**[Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16)** --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** horn +31 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+16 piercing damage plus 1d12 force damage **Primal Innate Spells** DC 34 - **3rd** [Humanoid Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=153) (at will) - **Constant (1st)** [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215) **Spirit Charge** Sié Goluo Strides twice, then makes a horn Strike. On a hit, Sié Goluo creates a shock wave that deals 4d12 force damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Creatures that fail their save are [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) away 5 feet (10 feet on a critical failure). **Spirit Steps** Sié Goluo ignores difficult terrain and doesn't trigger traps with his movement.
An Absent Relative Because sightings are so rare, not all Mwangi people believe Sié Goluo exists. Many regard him as merely another children's tale spun by their grandparents to soothe them to sleep. After all, there is very little physical evidence of his existence, he can't be reached through divination, and he disappears without a trace when he finishes what he set out to do. Some skeptics, when recounting tales of their death-defying exploits, might end their story with the phrase, “And Grandfather nowhere in sight!”
name: Sié Goluo

Solar ibis are beings of light sometimes called sun birds, or “winged luminaries” by the more scholarly inclined. They're tasked with guarding sacred places or items, although they sometimes also protect the living from places of incredible danger. Their appearance is always preceded by numerous small purple-winged birds with a splash of yellow on their crowns; these are avatars of the ibis that nest and congregate in areas it protects. The presence of these avatars is often the only way to track these reclusive and solitary creatures. Should the solar ibis choose to appear, these little birds scatter like wild fowl. The sun bird then manifests and bathes the area in a brilliant shower of light that can blind and startle unwary and unprepared creatures in its presence. The solar ibis appears as a magnificent, large bird surrounded by a halo of brilliant light. Its black feathers shimmer with all the colors of the spectrum, casting glossy rainbows across its plumage. It has a keen sense of insight, which it uses to pass judgment as a whole on whether those before it are worthy to access what it guards. It punishes those it finds unworthy with its cleansing might. Though considered sacred by most within the Mwangi Expanse, there are still some who hunt these glorious creatures; some seek the treasures that the ibis keep, while others seek the ibis themselves, dead or alive. Some believe that the feathers of a solar ibis, when burned, create an incense that can grant holy power to those that inhale it. Others believe that its bones and feet can be ground into a powder that can destroy demons and purify the undead. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Solar Ibis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1442) **Source** [The Mwangi Expanse](/Sources.aspx?ID=89) pg. 306 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 45 **Blinding Halo** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) 30 feet. The solar ibis's halo sheds bright light. Creatures that start their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 minute. A creature that critically fails its save is instead [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute. Creatures who successfully save are temporarily immune to this effect for 24 hours. The solar ibis can extinguish or resume the halo's light as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**Evasion** When a solar ibis succeeds at a Reflex save, it gets a critical success instead. --- **Speed** 10 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** beak +18, **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing **Melee** talon +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Ibis Dive** The solar ibis Flies up to double its fly Speed in a straight line, descends at least 10 feet, and then makes a talon Strike. **Light Shatter** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) The solar ibis detonates its halo, sending shards of burning light in all directions that deal 6d8 fire damage to all creatures in a 20-foot emanation (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The shards deal an extra 6d8 good damage against fiends and undead. The solar ibis can't use Light Shatter again for 1d4 rounds, and its Blinding Halo is extinguished during this time. The halo is restored automatically once the 1d4 rounds have passed.
A Severed Connection Scholars who know of [Atreia](/Deities.aspx?ID=101), the elemental lord of fire, suspect solar ibises might be related to the imprisoned demigod; Atreia is said to take the form of a three-headed ibis and was known for his holy, purifying flame. That this is mere coincidence seems too implausible to consider, but solar ibis don't acknowledge any connection to the demigod, leaving some to wonder if Atreia's imprisonment caused the sun birds to unconsciously forget their origins.
name: Solar Ibis

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Zinba](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1443)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Commonly known as ghost serpents, zinbas are rare, amphibious snakes with healing abilities. They're of similar size to other large snakes, such as pythons, but have a distinct bright blue coloring with red-and-black patterned stripes along their backs. This distinct coloration is easy to spot, but zinbas can swim through the wet mud and soft earth of their homes to avoid being tracked by all but the most skilled pursuers. This elusiveness inspires their “ghost serpent” moniker.

 A zinba can metabolize a unique additive for its venom that converts it from a deadly poison to a healing agent, which makes zinbas particularly valuable in the eyes of healers and poachers. There is a high demand for zinbas in many markets; live serpents fetch a hefty sum, but zinba eggs are especially valuable, as zinbas reared in captivity are less willful and more likely to eat in confinement. Their elusiveness, however, leads many to search for their dens instead, which are large pools of water among remote wetlands or underground hollows near bodies of water. The water of a zinba's pool also has minor but valuable healing qualities, but removing water from a den causes it to lose its healing properties after a few hours, so most who find a den attempt to immediately make use of it.

 Even finding dens is a difficult prospect, since zinbas are particularly intelligent for animals and can recognize signs of travelers near their favored hiding places. When there are signs of danger, a zinba collapses its lair and flees to create another den elsewhere.

In addition, the restorative attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against any [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) affecting the creature with a counteract modifier of +22. A creature that receives the restorative is temporarily immune to the zinba's restorative for 24 hours.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Zinba Dens</title>

The water in a zinba's den is suffused with its healing properties, making it as valuable as a zinba itself. Creatures that drink from these waters gain the curative effects of Zinba Restoration. This water can be placed into a container and removed from the den, but the curative effects fade 1d4 hours after removing the water. After a creature drinks water from a zinba's den, it is temporarily immune to the water's effects and the Zinba Restoration of the resident zinba for 24 hours. 
</aside>

markdown:
Wendlyn grew up in Otari and knows just about everyone in it. This means she knows which people are safe to target with her petty larceny. She's been on the wrong side of Captain Longsaddle of the Otari town guard for as long as she can remember, but she keeps away from the sorts of crimes that would call for serious punishment, or that would seriously hurt anyone. The people of Otari are immune to her thefts but not her pranks; she's fond of hiding animals where they'll surprise people or livening up a celebration with some unexpected fireworks. She considers it to be all in good fun. Captain Longsaddle would definitely disagree, as would her younger half-sister, the pious acolyte Lisavet. Wendlyn is an excellent person to become acquainted with in Otari, as she knows a fair share of the town's secrets. Her lifelong inclination toward theft also makes her just the right person to disable a tricky lock or disarm a complicated trap. She's eager to join just about any escapade, illicit or otherwise, as long as no one seems likely to get seriously hurt. Plot Hooks Game Masters might consider the following plot hooks as a means of integrating Wendlyn into any Pathfinder Second Edition game.
  • Wendlyn needs the heroes to help her recover a hand mirror she stole from an abandoned house and then fenced, as she didn't realize its presence was keeping a ghost confined to the old house. She hopes to recover the mirror from the fence and get it back into the house before anyone's hurt.
  • One of the heroes is an alibi who can prove Wendlyn wasn't at the scene of a crime that Captain Longsaddle is certain she committed. The heroes must work to prove the thief's innocence—in this particular matter, at least.
  • The heroes have come across a strange puzzle box that defies all attempts to open it, but an expert locksmith like Wendlyn might be able to help.
  • Through her shady contacts, Wendlyn has heard of a plot to smuggle drugs into Otari's harbor late at night. She hopes to intercept the smugglers, dump the drugs into the harbor's waters, and teach the smugglers to stay out of her town.

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 2d4+8 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dart +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d4+4 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
foxfire +19 ([range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d4 electricity

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 27
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61)
- **1st**
[Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=266)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Ji-yook transforms into her human form. Using Change Shape counts as creating a disguise for the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) use of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5). Ji-yook can remain in her human form indefinitely, and she can shift back to her true kitsune form by using this action again.

**Foxfire Blast** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) Ji-yook makes a fist Strike. On a hit, the target takes an additional 2d4 electricity damage and is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) back 5 feet. On a critical hit, the target is pushed back and then knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

**Pinning Shot** <actions string="Single Action" />  Ji-yook pins a foe's clothing to a wall or floor or the ground. Ji-yook makes a dart Strike. If the attack hits, it deals no damage and instead pins the target to an adjacent surface. The target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and must spend an Interact action to attempt a DC 10 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to pull the dart free; it can't move from its space until it succeeds. If Ji-yook's dart Strike was a critical hit, the target must succeed at two separate Athletics checks to pull the dart free. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) while it remains immobilized in this way.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   Ji-yook's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

</column>
name: Ji-yook (Level 9)

<title level="1" pfs="">[Weapon Master](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1449)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The weapon master is a deadly expert with their weapon of choice. Whenever possible, the weapon master engages an opponent one on one, and they prefer to begin combat unarmed and use their Swift Blow to catch a foe unawares.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Weapon Master](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1449)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 22

**Perception** +23

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +24, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +23

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**
_+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [katana](/Weapons.aspx?ID=55)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +24 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 250

</row>

**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_katana_ +28 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=198), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d6+11 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_sling_ +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning

**Dueling Expertise**   The weapon master deals an extra 2d10 damage to creatures that are not adjacent to any allies or other foes.

**Swift Blow** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) **Requirement** The weapon master is not wielding a weapon; **Effect** The weapon master Interacts to draw a melee weapon without triggering reactions and makes a Strike with that weapon. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against this Strike. The weapon master deals an extra 2d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) with the Strike.

</column>
name: Weapon Master

<title level="1" pfs="">[Agile Warrior](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1450)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The agile warrior uses their speed to jump around the battlefield and create openings. Making use of their Tumbling Attack, the agile warrior likes to spring back and forth between targets every round.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Agile Warrior](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1450)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 23

**Perception** +25

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +24, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [sai](/Weapons.aspx?ID=59)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [shuriken](/Weapons.aspx?ID=79)_ (5)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 35 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +21 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 180

</row>

**Nimble Dodge** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The agile warrior is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker they can see; **Effect** The agile warrior gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_sai_ +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=112), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 2d4+9 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_shuriken_ +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d4+11 piercing

**Deadly Disarm**   The agile warrior deals 2d6 precision damage to a creature on a successful [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) attempt.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The agile warrior's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

**Tumbling Attack** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The agile warrior Strides up to their Speed. During this movement, they can move through the space of one creature without triggering reactions. When the agile warrior moves through a creature's space in this way, that creature becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of the agile warrior's turn. If the agile warrior ends their movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, they can make a melee Strike against that enemy

</column>
name: Agile Warrior
